title: TEST: Discord Connector Integration Tests
description:
# TEST: Discord Connector Integration Tests (Deterministic)


## Goal
Validate `fcp.discord` integration behavior (gateway + REST) under deterministic mocks, including event tainting and approval-gated writes.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Purpose
Integration tests for `fcp.discord` using:
- a deterministic mock Discord REST API (wiremock or in-process)
- a deterministic mock Discord Gateway (WebSocket server)

No real Discord calls are allowed.

## What this suite must prove
### 1) Gateway correctness
- IDENTIFY / RESUME handling
- heartbeat timing + ack handling
- sequence number tracking and persistence
- reconnect/backoff behavior is bounded

### 2) REST correctness
- request formation for send_message / reactions / channel list
- error mapping (401/403/429/5xx → FCP error taxonomy)
- Retry-After compliance

### 3) Singleton-writer state
- only the lease-holder maintains the gateway connection
- restart resumes safely (or fails closed if resume is unsafe)

### 4) FCP2 connector contract
- standard method surface end-to-end
- capability gating (deny without token, allow with token)
- events emitted as EventEnvelopes with correct topics
- inbound events tainted as external input

## Required adversity cases
- global rate limit
- bucketed rate limit
- malformed gateway frames
- mid-stream disconnect during event emission

## Logging requirements
- Structured JSON logs with correlation IDs.
- Never log bot tokens or raw message bodies by default.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic in CI.
- Failures are actionable (DecisionReceipt reason_code + evidence surfaced).

comments.author: Jeff Emanuel
comments.text: SunnyMoose: Discord integration tests complete. 23 tests covering: lifecycle (configure/handshake/health, introspect 9 ops), all 9 operations (send, edit, delete, get_channel, get_guild, trigger_typing, add_reaction, list_channels, create_thread), input validation (content >2000 chars, missing fields, thread name limits), capability gating (missing token, wrong cap), error taxonomy (401/429/500), self-check, shutdown. Also fixed PUT method bug in request_no_response() (was falling through to GET). fmt/clippy/all tests green.

title: Study: Clawdbot Connector Patterns - Synthesis & Recommendations
description:
# Study: Clawdbot Connector Patterns - Synthesis & Recommendations


## Goal
Study and synthesize Clawdbot connector patterns into actionable guidance for FCP2 connector design (security, state, tests).

## Objective

Synthesize findings from Discord and Telegram connector studies into actionable patterns and recommendations for FCP connector development.

## Synthesis Areas

### 1. Common Patterns Identified
- [ ] Shared architectural patterns
- [ ] Common abstractions
- [ ] Reusable utility functions
- [ ] Configuration patterns
- [ ] Error handling approaches

### 2. Platform-Specific Adaptations
- [ ] How patterns adapt to different APIs
- [ ] Platform-specific optimizations
- [ ] Unique challenges per platform
- [ ] Platform capability differences

### 3. Reusable Components Catalog

#### Core Infrastructure
- Connection management
- Retry/backoff utilities
- Rate limiter
- Event dispatcher
- Configuration loader

#### Message Processing
- Command parser
- Response builder
- Attachment handler
- Formatter utilities

#### State Management
- Session manager
- Cache layer
- Persistence adapter

### 4. Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- [ ] Identified code smells
- [ ] Scalability issues
- [ ] Security concerns
- [ ] Maintainability problems
- [ ] Testing difficulties

### 5. FCP Connector Design Recommendations

#### Architecture
- Recommended module structure
- Interface definitions
- Dependency injection patterns
- Plugin system design

#### Implementation Guidelines
- Coding standards
- Error handling strategy
- Logging requirements
- Testing requirements

#### Documentation Standards
- API documentation
- Configuration documentation
- Troubleshooting guides

## Deliverables

1. **FCP Connector Template** - Skeleton project with best practices
2. **Pattern Library** - Reusable patterns with examples
3. **Style Guide** - Coding and architecture standards
4. **Checklist** - Connector review checklist
5. **Migration Guide** - How to port existing connectors to FCP

## Success Criteria

- Clear, actionable recommendations
- Reusable code templates
- Documented patterns with rationale
- Identified gaps requiring new solutions
- Roadmap for connector development

## Dependencies

- Completion of Discord connector study
- Completion of Telegram connector study

## Time Estimate

- Synthesis: 3-4 hours
- Template creation: 2-3 hours
- Documentation: 2-3 hours
- Total: 7-10 hours

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

title: [POL] Diff + risk summary engine
description:
# [POL] Diff + risk summary engine

## Goal
Provide deterministic diffs and risk summaries between policy bundles.

## Deliverables
- Diff engine (stable ordering) for:
  - Zone ceilings, capability grants, roles, and resource bindings.
  - Network constraints expansions (hosts/ports/CIDR changes).
  - Approval/elevation/declassification rules.
- Risk summary with reason codes and severity tiers.

## Design Notes
- Output must be stable and canonical for reproducible reviews.
- Risk summary flags widening egress, new risky caps, and lowered integrity.

## Tests
- Unit tests for diff correctness and ordering stability.
- Golden snapshot tests for representative bundle pairs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Same inputs always yield identical diff output.
- Summary flags risky changes with clear reason codes.
notes:
[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00
close_reason: Implemented deterministic PolicyBundle diff missing_objects normalization + added deterministic/golden diff tests in fcp-core policy module

title: [IDEA-26] Source Diversity Enforcement + Metrics
description:
## Background
Spec requires source diversity to avoid single-source reconstruction. We need mechanical enforcement and metrics to prove diversity SLOs.

## Goals
- Track per-object symbol source diversity.
- Enforce minimum distinct sources for reconstruction when policy requires.
- Emit metrics and structured logs for coverage and diversity.

## Non-goals
- Full repair controller redesign (only diversity enforcement + metrics).

## Approach
- Extend symbol store metadata to track source node IDs.
- Add policy flag for `min_source_diversity` per zone.
- Integrate with repair loop to fetch from additional sources.

## Risks
- Extra network cost; ensure policy-gated and bounded.

## Success Criteria
- Single-source prevention triggers with stable reason codes.
- Metrics expose diversity coverage per object.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Single‑source reconstruction is denied when policy requires diversity.
- Diversity metrics are surfaced for each object.
notes:
[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00

title: fcp.docusign: Send for Signature
description:
# fcp.docusign: Send for Signature (FCP2)

## Goal
Transition envelopes from draft → sent, initiating the real-world signature workflow.

This is a high-impact side effect and must be approval-gated by policy.

## Scope
- Send envelope for signature.
- Resend notifications (optional; dangerous-ish).
- Void/cancel envelope (dangerous).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `docusign.envelopes.send` (dangerous)
- `docusign.envelopes.resend` (dangerous)
- `docusign.envelopes.void` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - recipient emails/names must never appear in logs
- For send/void:
  - require ApprovalToken when policy dictates (recommended default)
  - emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent with envelope_id + action

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `docusign.envelope.send`
  - `docusign.envelope.resend`
  - `docusign.envelope.void`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - send happy path
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Envelope send/void operations are mechanically authorized, explainable, and auditable.

title: fcp.monday: Monday.com Work OS Connector
description:
## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant Monday.com connector for work management (boards/items/updates) with strict capabilities and webhook/event ingestion.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in the connector’s manifest/cap map bead if present).
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)

- `operational`
- `streaming`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Request/Response + Webhook

## Overview
Flywheel Connector for Monday.com Work OS platform.

## Connector Profile
- **Value Score**: 66/100 (Tier 4)
- **Archetype**: Request-Response + Webhook
- **Platform**: Work OS
- **API Type**: GraphQL

## Operations
- Board CRUD (create, read, update, delete)
- Items management
- Columns configuration
- Groups organization
- Updates/comments
- Files attachments
- Docs integration
- Workspaces management

## Zone Architecture

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

- **board read**: Read-only access to boards, items, columns, groups
- **board write**: Create/update/delete items, columns, groups, updates
- **workspace admin**: Workspace-level administration, user management

## Dependencies
- fcp-core: Core connector infrastructure
- fcp-oauth: OAuth2 authentication flow
- fcp-graphql: GraphQL client and query building
- fcp-webhook: Webhook event handling
- fcp-telemetry: Metrics and observability

## Implementation Notes
- Monday.com uses GraphQL API exclusively
- Requires OAuth2 app registration in Monday.com marketplace
- Webhook subscriptions for real-time board/item updates
- Rate limiting: 10,000 complexity points per minute
- Pagination via cursor-based approach

## Technical Considerations
- GraphQL query complexity management
- Batch operations for bulk item updates
- File upload via separate REST endpoint
- Webhook signature verification
- Column type mappings (status, people, date, etc.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OAuth2 flow with Monday.com marketplace app
- [ ] Board CRUD operations via GraphQL
- [ ] Item lifecycle management
- [ ] Column and group operations
- [ ] Update/comment threading
- [ ] File attachment handling
- [ ] Workspace administration
- [ ] Webhook event subscriptions
- [ ] Rate limit compliance
- [ ] Comprehensive test coverage

title: fcp.1password: Credential Management Connector
description:
## Overview
Secure credential management connector for 1Password integration, enabling AI agents to safely access and manage secrets without exposing sensitive data.

## Core Capabilities

### Item Lookup (Read-Only by Default)
- Search vault items by name, tags, or categories
- Retrieve specific fields (username, URL, notes) without exposing passwords
- Read-only access as the default permission level
- Explicit elevation required for password retrieval

### Password Generation
- Generate strong passwords with configurable requirements
- Support for length, character sets, and complexity rules
- Memorable password generation (word-based)
- PIN generation for numeric-only contexts

### Entry Creation
- Create new login entries with generated passwords
- Support for secure notes, credit cards, identities
- Custom field support for application-specific data
- Automatic categorization and tagging

### Vault Organization
- List and navigate vault structure
- Move items between vaults
- Bulk tagging operations
- Archive and restore items

### Secure Note Access
- Read secure notes for configuration data
- Structured note parsing (YAML, JSON embedded)
- Document attachment retrieval
- Version history access

## Security Requirements

### Credential Protection
- **CRITICAL**: Never expose credentials in logs or output
- Mask all sensitive fields in debug output
- Use secure memory handling
- Clear credentials from memory after use

### Time-Limited Credential Leases
- Short-lived access tokens (5-minute default)
- Automatic credential rotation
- Lease renewal with re-authentication
- Audit trail for all credential access

## Implementation Notes
- Use 1Password CLI (op) or Connect API
- Support both personal and team accounts
- Service account integration for automation
- MFA handling for interactive sessions

## Testing Requirements
- Mock vault for unit tests
- Integration tests with test vault
- Security audit for credential handling
- Penetration testing for leak prevention

title: fcp.datadog: Datadog Observability Platform Connector
description:
# fcp.datadog - Datadog Observability Platform Connector (FCP2)

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 84/100** — Important Tier 2 connector (Observability family).

**Why Critical**: Observability is essential for autonomous operations. Agents that can understand system health, diagnose issues, and correlate incidents enable truly autonomous SRE workflows. Datadog is the **market leader in cloud observability**.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (Specialized + High Value)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Knowledge,    // Metrics, logs, traces queries
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // Monitor management, incidents
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Webhook events
]
```
- **Knowledge**: Query metrics, logs, traces, dashboards
- **Operational**: Monitor CRUD, incident management
- **Streaming**: Webhooks for alerts, incidents

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::Stateless
```
- **Stateless**: Query operations are independent
- Time-range based queries (no cursor)
- Monitor state managed by Datadog

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles API credentials
- Memory isolation for key protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Datadog API endpoints (region-specific)
    host_allow: vec\![
        "api.datadoghq.com".into(),       // US1
        "api.datadoghq.eu".into(),        // EU1
        "api.us3.datadoghq.com".into(),   // US3
        "api.us5.datadoghq.com".into(),   // US5
        "api.ap1.datadoghq.com".into(),   // AP1
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// Telemetry data is internal/trusted
metric_data.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::NONE;

// Log messages may contain user data
log_message.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;

// Webhook payloads are external
webhook_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `datadog.metrics.query` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.logs.search` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.traces.search` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.dashboards.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.monitors.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.monitors.write` | Dangerous | Alert configuration |
| `datadog.incidents.manage` | Dangerous | Incident lifecycle |
| `datadog.dashboards.write` | Risky | Dashboard changes |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Metrics & Monitoring
- Query metrics (Datadog query language)
- Access dashboards
- Alert management
- Anomaly detection

### Logging
- Log search and analysis
- Log pattern detection
- Error correlation

### APM & Tracing
- Distributed trace inspection
- Service dependency mapping
- Performance bottleneck identification

### Incident Management
- View and manage incidents
- Correlate with metrics/logs
- Postmortem data

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Show me CPU usage for the past hour"
"Find error logs from the checkout service"
"What traces show latency > 500ms?"
"List all triggered monitors"
"Create a monitor for high error rate"
"Get incident timeline"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Sentry Integration
**Unified error tracking:**
- Error correlation
- Performance impact analysis

### GitHub Integration
- Deploy events → metric correlation
- Release impact analysis

### Slack/Twilio Integration
- Alert escalation
- Incident notifications

### Linear/Jira Integration
- Incident → issue creation
- Performance issue tracking

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **REST API v1/v2**: Primary interface
- **Webhooks**: Alert and incident events

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Authentication
```rust
// API Key + Application Key
DD-API-KEY: {api_key}
DD-APPLICATION-KEY: {app_key}
```

### Query Language
```
avg:system.cpu.user{env:production} by {host}
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:ops:datadog:full` | Full access | All operations |
| `z:ops:datadog:read` | Read-only | Queries only |
| `z:ops:datadog:monitors` | Monitors | Alert management |

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `knowledge` — Telemetry queries
- `operational` — Monitor management
- `streaming` — Webhook events

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.datadoghq.com:443` (region-specific)

### Credential Injection
- API key + App key via egress proxy
- Never persist credentials

---

## Capability Model

### Query Operations
- `datadog.metrics.query` — Metric queries
- `datadog.logs.search` — Log search
- `datadog.traces.search` — Trace search
- `datadog.dashboards.read` — Dashboard data

### Management Operations
- `datadog.monitors.read` — List monitors
- `datadog.monitors.write` — Create/update monitors (Dangerous)
- `datadog.incidents.manage` — Incident lifecycle (Dangerous)

### Admin Operations
- `datadog.dashboards.write` — Dashboard changes

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Query Metrics
```json
POST /api/v1/query
{
  "from": 1609459200,
  "to": 1609545600,
  "query": "avg:system.cpu.user{env:production}"
}
```

### Search Logs
```json
POST /api/v2/logs/events/search
{
  "filter": {
    "query": "service:checkout status:error",
    "from": "now-1h",
    "to": "now"
  }
}
```

### List Monitors
```
GET /api/v1/monitor
```

### Create Monitor
```json
POST /api/v1/monitor
{
  "name": "High Error Rate",
  "type": "metric alert",
  "query": "avg(last_5m):sum:errors{service:api} > 100",
  "message": "Error rate exceeded threshold"
}
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Query construction
- Response parsing
- Time range handling
- Error handling

### Integration Tests
- Mock Datadog API responses
- Multi-query scenarios
- Webhook handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Datadog API calls in unit tests
- Metric queries work
- Log search works
- Trace search works
- Monitor CRUD works
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements satisfied

title: fcp.microsoft365: Microsoft 365 Suite Integration Connector
description:
# FCP Microsoft 365 Suite Integration Connector ## Overview Comprehensive enterprise connector for Microsoft 365 suite, providing unified access through Microsoft Graph API. Critical for enterprise adoption with support for Outlook, OneDrive, Excel, Word, OneNote, SharePoint, and MS Planner. ## Sub-Connectors Architecture ### 1. Outlook (Email & Calendar) #### Email Operations - **Read Messages**: Inbox, folders, search - **Send Messages**: Compose, reply, forward - **Manage Messages**: Move, delete, archive - **Attachments**: Upload, download, inline - **Mail Folders**: Create, organize, rules #### Calendar Operations - **Events**: Create, update, delete, RSVP - **Calendars**: Multiple calendar support - **Availability**: Free/busy lookup - **Scheduling**: Find meeting times - **Recurring Events**: Complex patterns #### Contacts - **Contact Management**: CRUD operations - **Contact Folders**: Organization - **Contact Groups**: Distribution lists ### 2. OneDrive (File Storage) #### File Operations - **Upload**: Simple and resumable - **Download**: Direct and streaming - **Copy/Move**: Within and across drives - **Delete**: Soft delete and restore - **Sharing**: Links, permissions #### Folder Management - **Create/Delete Folders** - **List Contents**: Pagination support - **Search**: Full-text search - **Delta Sync**: Track changes #### Special Folders - Documents, Pictures, Desktop - App-specific folders - Shared folders ### 3. Excel Online #### Workbook Operations - **Open/Create Workbooks** - **List Worksheets** - **Workbook Sessions**: Persistent editing #### Data Operations ```python # Read range GET /workbook/worksheets/{id}/range(address='A1:D10') # Update range PATCH /workbook/worksheets/{id}/range(address='A1:D10') { "values": [["A", "B"], ["C", "D"]] } ``` #### Table Operations - **Create Tables**: From ranges - **Add Rows**: Append data - **Sort/Filter**: Data manipulation - **Calculated Columns**: Formulas #### Charts & Pivots - Create charts from data - Pivot table operations ### 4. Word Online #### Document Operations - **Create Documents**: From templates - **Read Content**: Full text extraction - **Update Content**: Append, replace - **Export**: PDF, other formats #### Content Controls - Find and replace - Bookmark management - Comment operations ### 5. OneNote #### Notebook Operations - **List Notebooks**: Personal and shared - **Create Notebooks** - **Sections**: Create, organize - **Section Groups**: Nested organization #### Page Operations ```python # Create page with HTML content POST /sections/{id}/pages Content-Type: text/html Page Title

Heading

Content here

``` #### Content - Rich text with HTML - Images and attachments - Ink and handwriting - Tags and to-do items ### 6. SharePoint #### Site Operations - **List Sites**: All accessible sites - **Site Details**: Properties, permissions - **Subsites**: Create, manage #### Lists & Libraries - **Document Libraries**: File storage - **Custom Lists**: Structured data - **List Items**: CRUD operations - **Views**: Custom views #### Pages & News - **Site Pages**: Create, update - **News Posts**: Publishing - **Web Parts**: Content blocks ### 7. MS Planner #### Plan Operations - **Create Plans**: Within Groups - **List Plans**: User's plans - **Plan Details**: Buckets, tasks #### Task Operations ```python { "planId": "...", "bucketId": "...", "title": "Task title", "assignments": { "user-id": { "@odata.type": "#microsoft.graph.plannerAssignment", "orderHint": " !" } }, "dueDateTime": "2024-12-31T00:00:00Z" } ``` #### Bucket Management - Create/organize buckets - Move tasks between buckets - Bucket ordering ## Microsoft Graph API (Unified Endpoint) ### Base Configuration ```yaml graph_api: base_url: "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0" beta_url: "https://graph.microsoft.com/beta" scopes: - "User.Read" - "Mail.ReadWrite" - "Files.ReadWrite.All" - "Calendars.ReadWrite" - "Sites.ReadWrite.All" - "Tasks.ReadWrite" ``` ### Batch Requests ```python POST /$batch { "requests": [ {"id": "1", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/messages"}, {"id": "2", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/events"}, {"id": "3", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/drive/root/children"} ] } ``` ## OAuth 2.0 with Refresh Tokens ### Authorization Flow ``` 1. GET /authorize?client_id=...&scope=...&redirect_uri=... 2. User consents 3. POST /token (authorization_code grant) 4. Store refresh_token securely 5. POST /token (refresh_token grant) when access_token expires ``` ### Token Management ```yaml auth: type: "oauth2" client_id: "${AZURE_CLIENT_ID}" client_secret: "${AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET}" tenant_id: "${AZURE_TENANT_ID}" redirect_uri: "http://localhost:8080/callback" token_storage: type: "encrypted_file" path: "/secure/tokens.enc" auto_refresh: true refresh_buffer_seconds: 300 ``` ### Scopes by Sub-connector | Sub-connector | Required Scopes | |---------------|-----------------| | Outlook Mail | Mail.Read, Mail.ReadWrite, Mail.Send | | Outlook Calendar | Calendars.Read, Calendars.ReadWrite | | OneDrive | Files.Read, Files.ReadWrite, Files.ReadWrite.All | | Excel | Files.ReadWrite | | Word | Files.ReadWrite | | OneNote | Notes.Read, Notes.ReadWrite | | SharePoint | Sites.Read.All, Sites.ReadWrite.All | | Planner | Tasks.Read, Tasks.ReadWrite, Group.ReadWrite.All | ## Webhook Subscriptions (Real-time Events) ### Subscription Management ```python # Create subscription POST /subscriptions { "changeType": "created,updated,deleted", "notificationUrl": "https://webhook.example.com/m365", "resource": "/me/messages", "expirationDateTime": "2024-12-31T00:00:00Z", "clientState": "secret-validation-token" } ``` ### Supported Resources - `/me/messages`: Email changes - `/me/events`: Calendar changes - `/me/contacts`: Contact changes - `/drives/{id}/root`: OneDrive changes - `/sites/{id}/lists/{id}/items`: SharePoint changes - `/groups/{id}/planner/tasks`: Planner changes ### Webhook Handler ```python async def handle_webhook(request): # Validation challenge if 'validationToken' in request.query: return PlainTextResponse(request.query['validationToken']) # Process notifications for notification in request.json()['value']: resource = notification['resource'] change_type = notification['changeType'] # Process change... ``` ### Subscription Renewal - Max lifetime: 3 days (most resources) - Auto-renewal before expiration - Webhook validation handling ## Configuration Schema ```yaml fcp.microsoft365: tenant_id: "${AZURE_TENANT_ID}" client_id: "${AZURE_CLIENT_ID}" client_secret: "${AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET}" auth: type: "oauth2" redirect_uri: "http://localhost:8080/callback" token_cache: "/secure/token_cache.json" sub_connectors: outlook: enabled: true mail_folders: ["Inbox", "Sent Items"] calendar_sync: true onedrive: enabled: true root_path: "/Documents" excel: enabled: true word: enabled: true onenote: enabled: true sharepoint: enabled: true sites: ["https://tenant.sharepoint.com/sites/team"] planner: enabled: true webhooks: enabled: true endpoint_url: "https://webhook.example.com/m365" resources: - "/me/messages" - "/me/events" rate_limits: requests_per_second: 10 concurrent_connections: 5 ``` ## MCP Tool Definitions ### Outlook Tools - `outlook_list_messages`: List emails - `outlook_send_message`: Send email - `outlook_create_event`: Create calendar event - `outlook_get_availability`: Check free/busy ### OneDrive Tools - `onedrive_list_files`: List directory contents - `onedrive_upload_file`: Upload file - `onedrive_download_file`: Download file - `onedrive_share_file`: Create sharing link ### Excel Tools - `excel_read_range`: Read cell range - `excel_write_range`: Write to cells - `excel_create_table`: Create data table - `excel_run_function`: Execute formula ### SharePoint Tools - `sharepoint_list_sites`: List sites - `sharepoint_get_list_items`: Read list data - `sharepoint_create_list_item`: Add list item ### Planner Tools - `planner_list_tasks`: List user tasks - `planner_create_task`: Create new task - `planner_update_task`: Update task status ### Webhook Tools - `m365_create_subscription`: Create webhook - `m365_list_subscriptions`: List active webhooks - `m365_delete_subscription`: Remove webhook ## Enterprise Features ### Multi-tenant Support - Per-tenant configuration - Tenant isolation - Cross-tenant operations (with consent) ### Compliance & Governance - Audit logging - Data residency awareness - eDiscovery integration ### Delegated vs Application Permissions - User context operations - Background service operations - Admin consent flows ## Error Handling - Token refresh failures - Rate limit (429) handling - Throttling with Retry-After - Permission denied errors - Resource not found ## Implementation Phases ### Phase 1: Core Auth (Week 1-2) - [ ] OAuth 2.0 flow implementation - [ ] Token storage and refresh - [ ] Multi-tenant support - [ ] Permission management ### Phase 2: Outlook (Week 3-4) - [ ] Mail operations - [ ] Calendar operations - [ ] Contact operations ### Phase 3: OneDrive & Files (Week 5-6) - [ ] File operations - [ ] Excel integration - [ ] Word integration ### Phase 4: Collaboration (Week 7-8) - [ ] SharePoint integration - [ ] OneNote integration - [ ] Planner integration ### Phase 5: Real-time (Week 9-10) - title: fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research
description:
# fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a research-focused macro surface that helps agents extract **authentic user opinions** from Reddit in a structured, bounded way.

This is not “LLM analysis inside the connector”; it is data acquisition + packaging so agents can do downstream reasoning safely.

## Scope
### Research macros (compositions)
- `reddit.research.search_threads`:
  - input: query + subreddit scope + time window
  - output: ranked thread summaries (ids, titles, scores, timestamps)
- `reddit.research.collect_evidence`:
  - input: thread ids
  - output: bounded comment excerpts with provenance pointers (post/comment ids)

### Safety/bounds
- Explicit caps:
  - max threads
  - max comments per thread
  - max total bytes returned
- NSFW/quarantine policy must be explicit.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.read`
- `reddit.search`
- `reddit.research` (macro operations)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- All content is tainted.
- Structured logs include only ids + counts.

## Deliverables
- Canonical output types:
  - `ResearchThreadSummary`
  - `ResearchEvidenceBundle`
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.research.search_threads`
  - `reddit.research.collect_evidence`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - ranking determinism
  - bounds enforcement
  - NSFW/quarantine gating

## Acceptance criteria
- Research macros are deterministic, bounded, and mechanically authorized.

title: fcp.sentry: Event details and stack trace inspection
description:
# fcp.sentry: Event details and stack trace inspection (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable deep triage by fetching **event-level details** for an issue, including stack traces and relevant context, while maintaining strict secret/PII hygiene.

## Scope
### Read operations
- Fetch latest event for an issue (or a specific event id).
- Retrieve:
  - stack trace frames (module/function/line)
  - breadcrumbs
  - tags
  - environment + release
  - request/user context (PII: must be handled carefully)
- Optional: retrieve attachments/artifacts metadata (download is separate + bounded).

### Output shaping
- Return a **sanitized, structured** view that is useful for debugging:
  - include stable identifiers
  - include stack traces (code locations are ok)
  - redact known secret-like fields

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.events.read`
- `sentry.artifacts.read` (if attachments are exposed)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Event payloads are untrusted and may contain secrets/PII.
  - Do not log raw payloads.
  - Apply redaction before returning “rendered” textual summaries.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.event.get`
  - `sentry.event.latest_for_issue`
  - `sentry.event.stacktrace`
- Redaction strategy:
  - allowlist safe fields
  - redact common secret patterns
  - cap maximum returned payload size

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - stack trace parsing and normalization
  - redaction correctness (golden vectors)
  - size limits + early abort
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Returns are useful for debugging without leaking secrets/PII.
- Logs remain metadata-only with correlation IDs.

title: fcp.grafana: Open Source Observability Platform Connector
description:
# fcp.grafana - Open Source Observability Platform Connector (FCP2)

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 83/100** — Important Tier 2 connector (Observability family).

**Why Critical**: Grafana is the **leading open-source observability platform**, providing unified dashboards across metrics, logs, and traces. For organizations that cannot use Datadog (cost, self-hosted requirements, open-source preference), Grafana is the alternative. Essential for complete observability coverage.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Open Source Alternative)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Knowledge,    // Dashboard, metric queries
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // Alert management
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Webhook events
]
```
- **Knowledge**: Query dashboards, metrics, logs via data sources
- **Operational**: Alert rule management, annotation CRUD
- **Streaming**: Webhooks for alerts

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::Stateless
```
- **Stateless**: Query operations are independent
- Dashboard state managed by Grafana
- No sync cursors needed

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles API tokens
- Memory isolation for credential protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Grafana Cloud
    host_allow: vec\![
        "*.grafana.net".into(),
        "grafana.com".into(),
    ],
    // Self-hosted: explicitly configured hostname
    port_allow: vec\![443, 3000],  // 3000 for self-hosted
    deny_localhost: true,   // Override for self-hosted
    deny_private_ranges: true,  // Override for self-hosted
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// Telemetry data is internal/trusted
metric_data.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::NONE;

// Annotations may contain user input
annotation.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;

// Webhook payloads are external
webhook_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `grafana.dashboards.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `grafana.datasources.query` | Safe | Read-only queries |
| `grafana.alerts.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `grafana.annotations.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `grafana.annotations.write` | Risky | Creates markers |
| `grafana.dashboards.write` | Risky | Dashboard changes |
| `grafana.alerts.manage` | Dangerous | Alert configuration |
| `grafana.datasources.manage` | Dangerous | Data source config |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Dashboard & Visualization
- Query dashboard definitions
- Access panel data
- Read annotations
- Export dashboard JSON

### Data Source Queries
- Execute queries against connected data sources
- Prometheus metrics
- Loki logs
- Tempo traces

### Alerting
- List and manage alert rules
- View alert history
- Configure notification channels

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Show me the CPU dashboard for production"
"Query error rate from Prometheus"
"Find logs from the API service"
"List all triggered alerts"
"Add an annotation for this deployment"
"Export this dashboard as JSON"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Datadog Comparison
**Observability alternatives:**
- Grafana for open-source/self-hosted
- Datadog for fully managed
- Both needed for market coverage

### Prometheus/Loki Integration
- Native data source support
- Metric and log queries

### Sentry Integration
- Error rate visualization
- Alert correlation

### GitHub Integration
- Deploy annotations
- Release correlation

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **HTTP API**: Dashboard, alerts, data sources
- **Data Source Proxy**: Query through Grafana

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Authentication
| Mode | Use Case |
|------|----------|
| API Key | Service account |
| Service Account Token | Cloud |
| Basic Auth | Self-hosted (legacy) |

### Data Sources
- Prometheus
- Loki
- Tempo
- InfluxDB
- Elasticsearch
- Many more...

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:ops:grafana:full` | Full access | All operations |
| `z:ops:grafana:read` | Read-only | Queries and dashboards |
| `z:ops:grafana:alerts` | Alerts only | Alert management |

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `knowledge` — Dashboard and query access
- `operational` — Alert management
- `streaming` — Webhook events

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `*.grafana.net:443` (Cloud)
- Configured hostname (self-hosted)

### Credential Injection
- API tokens via egress proxy
- Service account tokens via CredentialId

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `grafana.dashboards.read` — Dashboard definitions
- `grafana.datasources.query` — Execute queries
- `grafana.alerts.read` — Alert rules
- `grafana.annotations.read` — Annotations

### Write Operations
- `grafana.dashboards.write` — Create/update dashboards
- `grafana.annotations.write` — Create annotations

### Admin Operations
- `grafana.alerts.manage` — Alert rule CRUD (Dangerous)
- `grafana.datasources.manage` — Data source config (Dangerous)

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Get Dashboard
```
GET /api/dashboards/uid/{uid}
```

### Query Data Source
```json
POST /api/ds/query
{
  "queries": [{
    "refId": "A",
    "datasourceId": 1,
    "expr": "rate(http_requests_total[5m])"
  }],
  "from": "now-1h",
  "to": "now"
}
```

### List Alerts
```
GET /api/alertmanager/grafana/api/v2/alerts
```

### Create Annotation
```json
POST /api/annotations
{
  "dashboardUID": "abc123",
  "time": 1609459200000,
  "text": "Deployment v1.2.3"
}
```

### Search Dashboards
```
GET /api/search?query=production
```

---

## Data Source Query Examples

### Prometheus
```
rate(http_requests_total{status="500"}[5m])
```

### Loki
```
{app="api"} |= "error"
```

### Tempo
```
{resource.service.name="checkout"} && status=error
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Request construction
- Response parsing
- Query formatting per data source
- Error handling

### Integration Tests
- Mock Grafana API responses
- Multi-data-source scenarios
- Dashboard export/import

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Grafana API calls in unit tests
- Dashboard read works
- Data source queries work
- Alert management works
- Annotation CRUD works
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements satisfied

title: fcp.grafana: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)
description:

Produce the full FCP2 manifest and capability map for this connector.

This bead is where we translate the connector’s functional surface (operations) into a **mechanically checkable** contract:
- operation schemas + schema IDs
- capability families per operation
- per-operation NetworkConstraints (default deny)
- sandbox profile requirements
- declared state model (stateless / singleton_writer / crdt)

## Required content (must be explicit)
- Operation list is exhaustive and stable for the current phase.
- Capability IDs DO NOT encode hostnames/ports.
- NetworkConstraints are minimal and per-operation.
- Risk tier for each operation (Safe / Dangerous).
- Explicit bounds: timeouts, max response sizes, pagination limits.

## Tests
- Add good/bad manifest examples for the compliance runner.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes static checks in `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`.
- Default-deny is enforced by manifest + capability requirements.

## FCP2 requirements

title: fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution
description:
# fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe visibility into Terraform module structure so agents can:
- inspect required providers/modules
- analyze dependency graph
- prepare plans with correct initialization

## Scope
- Run `terraform init` in a controlled, non-interactive mode (may download modules/providers).
- List required providers and versions.
- Enumerate modules and sources.
- Produce a canonical `ModuleGraph` summary.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.modules.read`
- `terraform.init` (dangerous-ish if it downloads content; policy-gated)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace
  - restrict process execution
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless module registries are explicitly allowed.
- No automatic downloads unless policy explicitly permits.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.providers.list`
  - `terraform.modules.graph`
  - Optional: `terraform.init`
- Canonical output type:
  - `ModuleGraph`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with fixtures for:
  - module parsing
  - provider list parsing
  - policy denial of init without explicit capability

## Acceptance criteria
- Module graph summaries are deterministic and safe-by-default.

title: fcp.airtable: Base and table discovery API
description:
# fcp.airtable: Base and Table Discovery API

## Goal
Implement Airtable discovery operations so agents can understand what data exists before attempting record CRUD.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.bases.list`
- `airtable.bases.get` (schema)
- `airtable.tables.list`
- `airtable.tables.get`
- `airtable.fields.list`

## Key requirements
- Capability gated (`airtable.schema.read`).
- Prefer stable identifiers:
  - support referencing fields by **field id** (stable across renames) and name (human-readable).
- Cache schema responses with bounded TTL to reduce API load.
- Strict input validation:
  - base/table identifiers canonicalized
  - reject ambiguous references

## Tests
- Mock-only API responses for:
  - schema changes (rename fields, change options)
  - permission failures
  - rate limits
- Logs are structured JSON (redacted).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema discovery is deterministic and stable across renames.
- Conformance/E2E runner can validate default deny + allow paths.

title: TEST: Lifecycle Management Unit/Integration Tests
description:
## Goal
Validate lifecycle management behaviors (activation, updates/rollback, revocation interactions) with comprehensive deterministic tests.

## Overview

Comprehensive test suite for Connector Lifecycle Management (y1q8).

## Test Categories

### 1. Activation Workflow Tests
- **Full activation sequence**: sandbox → secrets → handshake → caps → health
- **Phase failure handling**: Each phase can fail, verify proper cleanup
- **Partial activation recovery**: Resume from failed phase
- **Concurrent activation prevention**: Only one activation at a time

### 2. ConnectorState Machine Tests
- **State transitions**: Valid transitions between all states
- **Invalid transitions**: Reject illegal state changes
- **Persistence**: State survives restart
- **Audit logging**: Each transition logged

### 3. Crash Loop Detection Tests
- **Threshold detection**: N crashes in M seconds triggers rollback
- **Window sliding**: Old crashes age out of window
- **Reset on success**: Successful run resets counter
- **Edge cases**: Exactly N crashes, N-1 crashes

### 4. Rollback Tests
- **Automatic rollback**: Crash loop triggers rollback
- **Version restoration**: Previous version correctly restored
- **Rollback audit**: Event logged with details
- **Rollback failure handling**: What if previous version also fails?

### 5. Update Manager Tests
- **Staged rollout**: Updates roll out progressively
- **Version pinning**: Pinned versions not auto-updated
- **Pin override**: Owner can unpin
- **Pin persistence**: Survives restart

### 6. Health Monitoring Tests
- **Health check timing**: Checks run at configured interval
- **Failure threshold**: Multiple failures before unhealthy
- **Recovery detection**: Healthy after passing checks
- **Timeout handling**: Slow connectors handled correctly

## Logging Requirements

All tests emit structured JSON:
```json
{
  "test": "test_activation_full_sequence",
  "phase": "negotiating_handshake",
  "connector_id": "fcp.telegram",
  "prev_state": "Activating",
  "new_state": "Running",
  "result": "success",
  "duration_ms": 234
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All activation phases tested individually
- [ ] State machine exhaustively tested
- [ ] Crash loop detection comprehensive
- [ ] Rollback scenarios covered
- [ ] Version pinning tested
- [ ] Health monitoring tested
- [ ] Structured JSON logging in all tests

title: fcp.sendgrid: Transactional Email Connector
description:
# fcp.sendgrid Connector


## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant SendGrid connector for transactional email send and event ingestion with strict redaction and auditability.

Transactional email platform connector for Flywheel agent notifications and communications.

## Credential handling (FCP2)
- Prefer secretless connectors: use `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` and let the egress proxy inject credentials at the network boundary when possible.
- If the protocol requires credential materialization inside the connector (e.g., some DB wire protocols), secrets MUST remain in memory only, be time-bounded, never touch disk, and never appear in logs/errors.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in the connector’s manifest/cap map bead if present).
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)

- `operational`
- `streaming`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Request/Response + Webhook

## Overview

- **Value Score**: 58/100 (Tier 4)
- **Archetype**: Request-Response + Webhook
- **Platform**: SendGrid v3 API
- **Authentication**: API Key

## Core Operations

### Email Send Zone
- **send_email**: Send single transactional email
- **send_batch**: Send batch emails (up to 1000 recipients)
- **send_template**: Send using dynamic template
- **schedule_send**: Schedule email for future delivery
- **cancel_scheduled**: Cancel scheduled send

### Template Management Zone
- **list_templates**: List dynamic templates
- **get_template**: Get template by ID with versions
- **create_template**: Create new dynamic template
- **update_template**: Update template metadata
- **create_version**: Create new template version
- **activate_version**: Set active template version

### Contact Management Zone
- **add_contacts**: Add/update contacts (upsert)
- **search_contacts**: Search contacts with SGQL
- **get_contact**: Get contact by ID or email
- **delete_contacts**: Remove contacts
- **list_lists**: Get contact lists
- **add_to_list**: Add contacts to list

### Suppression Management
- **list_bounces**: Get bounce suppressions
- **list_blocks**: Get block suppressions
- **list_spam_reports**: Get spam report suppressions
- **list_unsubscribes**: Get global unsubscribes
- **add_suppression**: Add email to suppression list
- **remove_suppression**: Remove from suppression

### Statistics Zone
- **global_stats**: Get global email stats
- **category_stats**: Stats by category
- **mailbox_provider_stats**: Stats by provider
- **browser_stats**: Email client stats

### Inbound Parse (Webhook)
- **configure_parse**: Set up inbound parse webhook
- **parse_email**: Process inbound email webhook

## Architecture

### Zone Design
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              fcp.sendgrid Connector             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Email Send  │  │   Template Management    │ │
│  │    Zone      │  │         Zone             │ │
│  │              │  │                          │ │
│  │ • send       │  │ • templates              │ │
│  │ • batch      │  │ • versions               │ │
│  │ • schedule   │  │ • handlebars             │ │
│  └──────────────┘  └──────────────────────────┘ │
│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────┐ │
│  │   Contact    │  │      Suppression         │ │
│  │ Management   │  │      Management          │ │
│  │              │  │                          │ │
│  │ • contacts   │  │ • bounces                │ │
│  │ • lists      │  │ • blocks                 │ │
│  │ • segments   │  │ • spam reports           │ │
│  └──────────────┘  └──────────────────────────┘ │
│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Statistics  │  │     Inbound Parse        │ │
│  │    Zone      │  │       (Webhook)          │ │
│  │              │  │                          │ │
│  │ • global     │  │ • parse config           │ │
│  │ • category   │  │ • email processing       │ │
│  │ • provider   │  │ • attachment handling    │ │
│  └──────────────┘  └──────────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Dependencies
- `fcp-core`: Base connector traits and types
- `fcp-webhook`: Inbound parse webhook handling
- `fcp-ratelimit`: SendGrid rate limit management
- `fcp-telemetry`: Email delivery metrics

## Implementation Details

### SendGrid v3 API
```rust
pub struct SendGridConnector {
    client: HttpClient,
    api_key: SecretString,
    rate_limiter: RateLimiter,
    webhook_handler: WebhookHandler,
}

impl SendGridConnector {
    pub async fn send_email(&self, email: Email) -> Result<SendResponse>;
    pub async fn send_batch(&self, batch: BatchEmail) -> Result<BatchResponse>;
    pub async fn get_stats(&self, query: StatsQuery) -> Result<Stats>;
}
```

### Rate Limiting
- Global: 100 requests/second (starter), 600/sec (pro)
- Email send: 600/minute (free), 10000/minute (paid)
- Contact uploads: 3 concurrent jobs
- Implement exponential backoff on 429

### Webhook Signature Verification
```rust
impl WebhookVerifier for SendGridWebhook {
    fn verify(&self, payload: &[u8], signature: &str) -> Result<bool> {
        // ECDSA signature verification
        // Public key from SendGrid settings
    }
}
```

### Email Types
```rust
pub struct Email {
    pub personalizations: Vec<Personalization>,
    pub from: EmailAddress,
    pub subject: String,
    pub content: Vec<Content>,
    pub attachments: Option<Vec<Attachment>>,
    pub template_id: Option<String>,
    pub dynamic_template_data: Option<Value>,
    pub categories: Vec<String>,
    pub send_at: Option<i64>,
}

pub struct Personalization {
    pub to: Vec<EmailAddress>,
    pub cc: Option<Vec<EmailAddress>>,
    pub bcc: Option<Vec<EmailAddress>>,
    pub substitutions: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
    pub dynamic_template_data: Option<Value>,
}
```

## Agent Use Cases

### Notification Delivery
- Alert emails for agent events
- Status update notifications
- Error/exception reports
- Scheduled digest emails

### Communication Workflows
- User onboarding sequences
- Password reset emails
- Verification emails
- Transactional receipts

### Inbound Processing
- Parse incoming emails to agent
- Extract commands from email
- Process email attachments
- Auto-reply handling

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
- Email payload construction
- Template variable substitution
- Rate limit calculation
- Webhook signature verification

### Integration Tests
- Send test emails (sandbox mode)
- Template CRUD operations
- Contact management
- Stats retrieval

### Webhook Tests
- Inbound parse processing
- Event webhook handling
- Signature verification

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Send single and batch transactional emails
- [ ] Dynamic template support with Handlebars
- [ ] Contact list management with SGQL search
- [ ] Suppression list management (bounces, blocks, spam)
- [ ] Email statistics retrieval
- [ ] Inbound parse webhook handling
- [ ] Rate limiting with backoff
- [ ] Comprehensive error handling
- [ ] Sandbox mode for testing

- operation schemas + schema IDs
- capability families per operation

## Required content (must be explicit)

## Tests
- Add good/bad manifest examples for the compliance runner.

title: fcp.sendgrid: Provisioning Automation

## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

title: fcp.twilio: Voice calls with outbound and IVR support
description:
# fcp.twilio: Voice calls with outbound and IVR support (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable safe voice automation via Twilio:
- initiate outbound calls
- manage call status and termination
- generate constrained TwiML for IVR-style flows

Voice calls are high-impact side effects; treat as approval-gated by policy.

## Scope
### Outbound call control
- Create outbound call to a destination.
- Query call status.
- Hang up a call.
- Optional: conference creation/management (defer if too large).

### TwiML (constrained)
- Generate TwiML responses from a safe template set:
  - `<Say>`, `<Gather>`, `<Dial>`, `<Redirect>`
- Explicitly avoid arbitrary injection of external URLs/scripts.

### Recording/transcription (optional)
- Accessing recordings is sensitive; treat as dangerous with explicit capability.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `twilio.voice.call` (dangerous)
- `twilio.voice.read`
- `twilio.voice.hangup` (dangerous)
- `twilio.voice.twiml.generate`
- Optional: `twilio.voice.recordings.read` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Twilio API host(s).
- For call initiation/termination:
  - require ApprovalToken when policy dictates
  - emit receipts + audit events with call SID + destination hash (never raw phone number)
- Strict log redaction for phone numbers, call recordings, and transcribed content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `twilio.voice.call`
  - `twilio.voice.get`
  - `twilio.voice.hangup`
  - `twilio.voice.twiml.render`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - call create/get/hangup request formation
  - TwiML template rendering
  - approval gating paths
  - log redaction

## Acceptance criteria
- Voice operations are mechanically authorized, explainable, and audited.
- TwiML generation is constrained and cannot be used as an injection vector.

title: fcp.zendesk: SLA Tracking
description:
# fcp.zendesk: SLA Tracking (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose SLA state and warnings so agents can:
- monitor SLA compliance
- prioritize tickets at risk of breach
- trigger escalation workflows safely

## Scope
### Read
- Retrieve SLA policy metadata (where permitted).
- For a ticket:
  - current SLA status
  - time-to-breach estimates

### Event ingestion (optional)
- Ingest SLA breach warning events via webhook if Zendesk supports it.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `zendesk.sla.read`
- `zendesk.webhook.ingest` (if SLA events are delivered)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- SLA events are tainted external inputs.
- No PII in logs.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `zendesk.sla.ticket_status`
  - Optional: `zendesk.webhook.sla_event_ingest`
- Reason codes for escalation suggestions (not auto-actions).

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - SLA status parsing
  - webhook replay/idempotency (if events exist)

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents can reliably detect SLA risk without leaking ticket content.

title: FCP V2 Connector Compliance Checklist (NORMATIVE)
description:
# FCP V2 Connector Compliance Checklist (NORMATIVE)

## Goal
Provide a single authoritative, V2-only checklist of all mechanical connector compliance requirements so new connectors can be reviewed for conformance without re-reading the spec/docs.

## Purpose
This checklist captures all NORMATIVE requirements from FCP Specification V2 and docs/fcp_model_connectors_rust.md that every connector MUST satisfy. Use this as a reference when creating or reviewing connector beads.

---

## 1. Connector Definition (NORMATIVE)

### Required Fields
Every connector MUST define:
- `id: ConnectorId` — Unique identifier (canonical: lowercase ASCII, `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9._:-]*$`)
- `name: String` — Human-readable name
- `version: Version` — Semantic version
- `format: ConnectorFormat` — Native or WASI
- `archetypes: Vec<ConnectorArchetype>` — From closed set
- `operations: Vec<Operation>` — All operations provided
- `events: Vec<EventType>` — Events emitted (if any)
- `required_caps: Vec<CapabilityId>` — Capabilities connector requires
- `optional_caps: Vec<CapabilityId>` — Optional capabilities
- `forbidden_caps: Vec<CapabilityId>` — Capabilities connector must NOT have

### ConnectorArchetype (Closed Set)
```rust
pub enum ConnectorArchetype {
    Bidirectional, // Sends and receives messages
    Streaming,     // Emits events (read-only)
    Operational,   // Executes operations (write)
    Storage,       // Stores/retrieves data
    Knowledge,     // Provides knowledge/search
}
```

### ConnectorFormat (NORMATIVE)
```rust
pub enum ConnectorFormat {
    Native, // Requires OS-level sandboxing (seccomp, landlock, AppArmor)
    Wasi,   // RECOMMENDED for high-risk connectors (financial, credentials, external API)
}
```

**Guidance:**
- WASI format SHOULD be used for SafetyTier::Dangerous operations
- WASI provides memory isolation, capability-gated hostcalls, cross-platform consistency

---

## 2. Connector State Model (NORMATIVE)

### State Model Declaration
Every connector MUST declare one:
```rust
pub enum ConnectorStateModel {
    Stateless,           // No mesh-persisted state
    SingletonWriter,     // Exactly one writer via Lease
    Crdt { crdt_type },  // Multi-writer via CRDT (rare; justify)
}
```

### State Persistence Rules
- Connectors with polling/cursors/dedup caches MUST externalize state to mesh
- Local `$CONNECTOR_STATE` is cache only
- Authoritative state lives as mesh objects
- Single-writer connectors MUST use Lease for fencing

### Fork Detection (NORMATIVE for singleton_writer)
- Two `ConnectorStateObject` with same `prev` = SAFETY INCIDENT
- MUST pause connector execution
- MUST log fork event for audit

---

## 3. Zone Architecture (NORMATIVE)

### Single-Zone Binding
- Connector instance MUST bind to exactly ONE zone
- Cross-zone access requires explicit policy

### Standard Zone Hierarchy
```
z:owner         → Full owner access
z:private       → Personal data
z:work          → Work services
z:project:<name>→ Per-project isolation
z:community     → Semi-trusted communities
z:public        → Public/untrusted inputs
```

### Zone Rules
1. Default deny — If capability not granted to zone, impossible to invoke
2. No cross-connector calling — All composition through Gateway

---

## 4. Capability System (NORMATIVE)

### Capability Definition
```rust
pub struct Capability {
    pub id: CapabilityId,
    pub risk_level: RiskLevel,      // Low, Medium, High, Critical
    pub safety_tier: SafetyTier,    // Safe, Risky, Dangerous, Forbidden
    pub idempotency: IdempotencyClass,
    pub requires_approval: ApprovalMode,
    pub audit_level: AuditLevel,
    pub agent_hint: AgentHint,
}
```

### SafetyTier (NORMATIVE)
```rust
pub enum SafetyTier {
    Safe,      // No approval needed
    Risky,     // Policy-based approval
    Dangerous, // Interactive human approval
    Forbidden, // Never allowed
}
```

### ApprovalMode (NORMATIVE)
```rust
pub enum ApprovalMode {
    None,              // No approval needed
    Policy,            // Policy-based auto-approval
    Interactive,       // Human approval required
    ApprovalRequired,  // Requires ApprovalToken
}
```

---

## 5. NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)

### Default-Deny Allowlists
```rust
pub struct NetworkConstraints {
    pub host_allow: Vec<String>,     // Allowed hostnames
    pub port_allow: Vec<u16>,        // Allowed ports
    pub ip_allow: Vec<IpAddr>,       // Optional explicit IPs
    pub cidr_deny: Vec<String>,      // NORMATIVE defaults below
    pub deny_localhost: bool,        // NORMATIVE default: true
    pub deny_private_ranges: bool,   // NORMATIVE default: true
    pub deny_tailnet_ranges: bool,   // NORMATIVE default: true
    pub deny_ip_literals: bool,      // NORMATIVE default: true
    pub require_sni: bool,           // Enforce SNI match
    pub spki_pins: Vec<String>,      // Optional SPKI pins
    pub require_host_canonicalization: bool, // NORMATIVE default: true
}
```

### NORMATIVE CIDR Deny Defaults
- `127.0.0.0/8` (localhost)
- `::1` (IPv6 localhost)
- `169.254.0.0/16` (link-local)
- `10.0.0.0/8` (RFC1918)
- `172.16.0.0/12` (RFC1918)
- `192.168.0.0/16` (RFC1918)
- `100.64.0.0/10` (Tailnet ranges)

### Host Canonicalization (NORMATIVE)
- Hostnames MUST be lowercase
- IDNA2008 encoded
- No trailing dot
- Implementations MUST reject non-canonical forms

---

## 6. Egress Proxy & Credential Injection (NORMATIVE)

### Secretless Connectors (RECOMMENDED)
- Credentials injected by egress proxy at network boundary
- Raw secrets never enter connector memory
- Credentials declared via `credential_allow` in `CapabilityConstraints`

### CredentialObject (NORMATIVE)
```rust
pub struct CredentialObject {
    pub credential_id: CredentialId,
    pub secret_id: SecretId,
    pub apply: CredentialApply,     // HttpHeader or QueryParam
    pub host_allow: Vec<String>,    // Defense-in-depth binding
}
```

### EgressHttpRequest (NORMATIVE)
```rust
pub struct EgressHttpRequest {
    pub method: String,
    pub url: String,
    pub headers: Vec<(String, String)>,
    pub body: Vec<u8>,
    pub credential: Option<CredentialId>, // Proxy injects this
}
```

---

## 7. Provenance and Taint (NORMATIVE)

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
bitflags! {
    pub struct TaintFlags: u32 {
        const NONE            = 0;
        const PUBLIC_INPUT    = 1 << 0;  // z:public, web
        const EXTERNAL_INPUT  = 1 << 1;  // Paired external identities
        const UNVERIFIED_LINK = 1 << 2;  // URLs/attachments not scanned
        const USER_SUPPLIED   = 1 << 3;  // Direct human input
        const PROMPT_SURFACE  = 1 << 4;  // Content interpreted by LLM
    }
}
```

### Taint Enforcement (NORMATIVE)
- Public-tainted input cannot directly drive Dangerous operations
- If `effective_taint() != NONE` and `operation.safety_tier >= Risky`, ApprovalToken required

### SanitizerReceipt (NORMATIVE)
- Machine-verifiable proof of sanitization
- Clears specific taints with evidence
- Connector version tracked for CVE auditing

---

## 8. Testing Requirements (NORMATIVE)

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- MUST NOT make real API calls
- Use wiremock or similar for HTTP mocking
- Test error conditions and rate limits
- Golden vectors for canonical serialization

### Integration Tests
- Mock external services
- Test multi-component scenarios
- Verify error handling

### E2E Compliance
- Run shared compliance runner
- Validate:
  - Default deny enforced
  - NetworkConstraints enforced
  - Receipts/audit for dangerous ops
  - Structured logs present
- Capture evidence bundle

---

## 9. Audit and Observability (NORMATIVE)

### AuditEvent Requirements
- All writes/dangerous ops MUST emit AuditEvent
- Correlation IDs for tracing
- No secrets in logs
- Structured JSON format

### Logging Requirements
- Never log tokens, passwords, PII
- Use reason codes for denials
- Include correlation IDs

---

## 10. Manifest Requirements

### Required Manifest Sections
```toml
[connector]
id = "fcp.<service>"
name = "Human Name"
version = "1.0.0"
archetypes = ["operational"]  # From closed set

[connector.state]
model = "singleton_writer"  # or "stateless" or "crdt"
state_schema_version = "1"

[connector.network]
host_allow = ["api.service.com"]
port_allow = [443]
deny_localhost = true
deny_private_ranges = true
```

### Sandbox Profile (NORMATIVE)
- Strict: No raw sockets, egress proxy only
- Moderate: Limited syscalls, proxy required
- Permissive: Full syscalls (avoid for sensitive connectors)

---

## Compliance Checklist Summary

### Connector Definition
- [ ] ConnectorId is canonical (lowercase, ASCII, regex-valid)
- [ ] Archetypes from closed set only
- [ ] Format specified (Native or WASI)
- [ ] WASI used for Dangerous operations

### State Management
- [ ] State model declared (Stateless/SingletonWriter/Crdt)
- [ ] State externalized to mesh (not local-only)
- [ ] Fork detection implemented for singleton_writer

### Security
- [ ] NetworkConstraints default-deny
- [ ] All NORMATIVE CIDR denies applied
- [ ] Host canonicalization enforced
- [ ] Credential injection via egress proxy (secretless)
- [ ] Taint tracking for external inputs

### Capabilities
- [ ] SafetyTier assigned per operation
- [ ] ApprovalMode defined
- [ ] AgentHints provided

### Testing
- [ ] Unit tests mock-only (no real API calls)
- [ ] E2E compliance runner passes
- [ ] Golden vectors for serialization

### Observability
- [ ] AuditEvents for dangerous ops
- [ ] Structured JSON logs
- [ ] No secrets logged

---

## References
- FCP_Specification_V2.md (canonical spec)
- docs/fcp_model_connectors_rust.md (connector guide)
- STANDARD: Connector Spec Template (flywheel_connectors-lszk.5)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checklist is V2-only and covers all NORMATIVE connector requirements from the spec + model connector guide.
- Checklist is kept in sync with `flywheel_connectors-1n78.1` and is used as the human-readable companion to the mechanical compliance runner.
- Each section is written so reviewers can mechanically verify compliance without relying on implicit assumptions.
close_reason: Created docs/STANDARD_Connector_Compliance.md - comprehensive V2-only compliance checklist covering all 10 NORMATIVE requirement categories: Connector Definition, State Model, Zone Architecture, Capability System, NetworkConstraints, Egress/Credentials, Provenance/Taint, Testing, Audit/Observability, and Manifest. Includes quick-reference checklist for reviewers and acceptance criteria. Unblocks 5 downstream beads including connector scaffold CLI and meta-connector EPICs.

title: fcp.google-ai: Google AI/Gemini Provider Connector
description:
# fcp.google-ai - Google AI/Gemini Provider Connector (FCP2)

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 94/100** — Critical Tier 1 connector (AI Provider family).

**Why Critical**: Google AI (Gemini) offers unique capabilities including **massive context windows** (up to 2M tokens), **native multimodal understanding** (video, audio, images), and tight integration with Google ecosystem. Essential for diverse AI workloads.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Foundation)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 1 Foundation (immediate)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // Generation requests
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Token-by-token streaming
]
```
- **Operational**: Request/response generation, embeddings
- **Streaming**: Server-sent events for progressive output

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::Stateless
```
- **Stateless**: Each request independent
- No cursor/sync state needed
- Token counting for cost tracking (ephemeral)

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles API credentials
- Memory isolation for key protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Google AI API endpoints
    host_allow: vec\![
        "generativelanguage.googleapis.com".into(),
        // Vertex AI (enterprise)
        "aiplatform.googleapis.com".into(),
        "{region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com".into(),
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// User prompts are tainted
user_prompt.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED
    | TaintFlags::PROMPT_SURFACE;

// Model outputs may contain unverified content
model_response.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;

// Grounded responses (Google Search) are external
if response.grounding_used {
    response.provenance.taint |= TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;
}
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `google-ai.models.list` | Safe | Read-only metadata |
| `google-ai.generate` | Risky | Consumes quota, may produce harmful content |
| `google-ai.generate.stream` | Risky | Same as generate |
| `google-ai.embeddings` | Safe | Deterministic transformation |
| `google-ai.chat` | Risky | Stateful generation |
| `google-ai.files.upload` | Risky | Multimodal input storage |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Gemini Unique Strengths
- **Massive context windows** — Process entire codebases, long documents
- **Native multimodal** — Video understanding, audio processing
- **Google Search grounding** — Real-time information access
- **Vertex AI integration** — Enterprise deployment options

### Core Operations
- Text generation (non-stream + streaming)
- Multi-turn chat conversations
- Embeddings generation
- Multimodal inputs (images, video, audio, PDFs)

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Analyze this hour-long video and summarize key points"
"Process this entire codebase in one context"
"Generate embeddings for this document corpus"
"Use Google Search to ground this response"
"Analyze this PDF document with images"
"Compare these two large documents"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### LLM Router Integration
**Model selection layer:**
- Route to Gemini for long-context tasks
- Fall back to Gemini for multimodal
- Cost optimization across providers

### Vector DB Integration
- Gemini embeddings → vector storage
- Semantic search over large corpora
- Document similarity

### Agent Mail Integration
- Long document processing
- Video/audio message analysis
- Multimodal communication support

### YouTube Integration
- Video transcription + understanding
- Content analysis pipelines
- Combined video + text workflows

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **Generative Language API**: Primary interface
- **Vertex AI**: Enterprise/regional deployment
- **Files API**: Multimodal uploads

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling
- `async-stream` — SSE streaming
- `base64` — Image encoding

### Model Variants
| Model | Context | Multimodal | Use Case |
|-------|---------|------------|----------|
| gemini-1.5-pro | 2M tokens | Yes | Long documents |
| gemini-1.5-flash | 1M tokens | Yes | Fast, cost-effective |
| gemini-1.0-pro | 32k tokens | Limited | Legacy |
| text-embedding-004 | 2048 tokens | No | Embeddings |

### Rate Limits
```rust
RateLimits {
    requests_per_minute: 60,    // Free tier
    tokens_per_minute: 1000000,  // Varies by plan
    concurrent_requests: 5,
}
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:ai:google:full` | Full access | All operations |
| `z:ai:google:generate` | Generation only | No embeddings |
| `z:ai:google:embeddings` | Embeddings only | No generation |

### Cost Considerations
- Token-based pricing
- Video/audio charged by duration
- Budget controls at zone level

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — Request/response generation
- `streaming` — Progressive token output

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `generativelanguage.googleapis.com:443`
- `{region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com:443` (Vertex)

### Credential Injection
- API key via egress proxy (simple)
- OAuth2 service account (Vertex AI)
- Never persist credentials

---

## Capability Model

### Generation Operations
- `google-ai.generate` — Text generation
- `google-ai.generate.stream` — Streaming generation
- `google-ai.chat` — Multi-turn conversation
- `google-ai.embeddings` — Vector embeddings

### Metadata Operations
- `google-ai.models.list` — Available models
- `google-ai.models.get` — Model details

### Multimodal Operations
- `google-ai.files.upload` — Upload for processing
- `google-ai.files.delete` — Remove uploaded files

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Generate Content
```json
POST /v1beta/models/gemini-1.5-pro:generateContent
{
  "contents": [{"parts": [{"text": "Explain quantum computing"}]}],
  "generationConfig": {
    "maxOutputTokens": 8192,
    "temperature": 0.7
  }
}
```

### Streaming Generation
```json
POST /v1beta/models/gemini-1.5-pro:streamGenerateContent?alt=sse
{
  "contents": [{"parts": [{"text": "Write a story"}]}]
}
```

### Generate Embeddings
```json
POST /v1beta/models/text-embedding-004:embedContent
{
  "content": {"parts": [{"text": "Hello world"}]}
}
```

### Multimodal (Image)
```json
POST /v1beta/models/gemini-1.5-pro:generateContent
{
  "contents": [{
    "parts": [
      {"text": "Describe this image"},
      {"inline_data": {"mime_type": "image/jpeg", "data": "base64..."}}
    ]
  }]
}
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Request serialization
- Response parsing
- Streaming SSE parsing
- Token counting
- Rate limit handling

### Integration Tests
- Mock API responses
- Multi-turn conversations
- Multimodal inputs
- Error scenarios

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Budget enforcement
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Google AI API calls in unit tests
- Text generation functional
- Streaming works correctly
- Embeddings generation works
- Multimodal inputs supported
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements satisfied

## Goal

## Scope

## Acceptance criteria
