title: TEST: fcp-core Unit Test Suite
description:
# TEST: fcp-core Unit Test Suite


## Goal
Validate `fcp-core` primitives (types, IDs, object headers) with golden vectors and invariant tests.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for the FCP core library - the foundation all connectors depend on.

## Test Categories

### Zone System
- [ ] ZoneId parsing and validation
- [ ] Zone hierarchy traversal
- [ ] Trust level calculations
- [ ] Zone boundary enforcement
- [ ] Cross-zone access denial

### Capability System
- [ ] CapabilityId parsing
- [ ] Capability matching and subsumption
- [ ] Constraint validation
- [ ] Resource limit enforcement

### Capability Token (FCT)
- [ ] Token serialization/deserialization
- [ ] Ed25519 signature creation
- [ ] Signature verification (valid/invalid/expired)
- [ ] Token field validation
- [ ] Expiration handling
- [ ] Audience verification

### Protocol Types
- [ ] HandshakeRequest/Response serialization
- [ ] InvokeRequest/Response serialization
- [ ] SubscribeRequest/Response serialization
- [ ] All message types round-trip correctly

### Error Handling
- [ ] FcpError code mapping
- [ ] Error serialization
- [ ] Retryable error detection
- [ ] AI recovery hint generation

### Provenance
- [ ] Taint label propagation
- [ ] Origin tracking
- [ ] Causal chain validation

### Introspection
- [ ] OperationInfo serialization
- [ ] EventInfo serialization
- [ ] JSON Schema generation
- [ ] AI hints formatting

### Connector Traits
- [ ] FcpConnector trait default implementations
- [ ] BaseConnector helper methods
- [ ] Metrics collection accuracy

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/core/`
- `zone_id_valid.json` - Valid ZoneId examples with expected parsing
- `zone_id_invalid.json` - Invalid ZoneIds that should be rejected
- `capability_token.cbor` - Serialized capability token
- `object_header.cbor` - Canonical ObjectHeader

## Fuzz Targets
- `fuzz_zone_id_parse` - ZoneId parsing with arbitrary input
- `fuzz_capability_token` - CapabilityToken deserialization
- `fuzz_object_header` - ObjectHeader parsing

## Logging Requirements
All tests MUST emit structured JSON logs:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "level": "info|warn|error",
  "test_name": "test_zone_id_parsing",
  "module": "fcp-core",
  "phase": "setup|execute|verify|teardown",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "duration_ms": 5,
  "assertions_passed": 10,
  "assertions_failed": 0
}
```

### For Zone Tests
```json
{
  "zone_id": "z:work",
  "zone_hash": "...",
  "parent_zone": "z:owner"
}
```

### For Capability Tests
```json
{
  "token_id": "...",
  "issuer_kid": "...",
  "grant_count": 3,
  "expiry": "RFC3339"
}
```

## CI Integration
```bash
cargo test -p fcp-core -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee test_output.log
jq -s '.' test_output.log > test_results.json
```

## Coverage Target: >95% (core library is critical)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All core types have round-trip tests
- Golden vectors match exactly
- Fuzz targets run without panics
- Structured logs produced for all tests
- No regressions in coverage
close_reason: Completed fcp-core unit test suite: 182 tests covering capability, connector, error, event, health, object, and protocol modules

title: TEST: Provenance/Taint Unit Tests (Label Propagation, Approval Enforcement, Sanitizer Receipts)
description:
# TEST: Provenance/Taint Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that information flow controls work correctly and cannot be bypassed.

## Test Categories

### Provenance Field Handling
- [ ] origin_zone correctly tracked
- [ ] current_zone updated on zone crossing
- [ ] integrity_label correctly propagated
- [ ] confidentiality_label correctly propagated
- [ ] taints accumulated correctly

### Taint Flags
- [ ] PUBLIC_INPUT flag applied to public data
- [ ] EXTERNAL_INPUT flag applied to external API responses
- [ ] UNVERIFIED_LINK flag applied to unverified references
- [ ] USER_SUPPLIED flag applied to user inputs
- [ ] PROMPT_SURFACE flag applied to data reaching prompts
- [ ] Flags accumulate (union) correctly

### Label Merge Rules
- [ ] integrity = MIN(input_integrities) - verified
- [ ] confidentiality = MAX(input_confidentialities) - verified
- [ ] Property tests: merge is associative and commutative
- [ ] Edge cases: empty inputs, single input, many inputs

### Integrity Flow (Biba Model)
- [ ] Down-flow (high→low integrity): allowed without approval
- [ ] Up-flow (low→high integrity): requires Elevation approval
- [ ] Missing elevation approval → rejection with reason_code
- [ ] Valid elevation approval → allow

### Confidentiality Flow (Bell-LaPadula Model)
- [ ] Up-flow (low→high confidentiality): allowed without approval
- [ ] Down-flow (high→low confidentiality): requires Declassification approval
- [ ] Missing declassification approval → rejection
- [ ] Valid declassification approval → allow

### ApprovalToken Verification
- [ ] scope = Elevation: validates integrity transition
- [ ] scope = Declassification: validates confidentiality transition
- [ ] from_label/to_label binding enforced
- [ ] Time validity (iat/exp) enforced
- [ ] Signature verification succeeds/fails correctly

### SanitizerReceipt Verification
- [ ] Receipt covers input object IDs
- [ ] Receipt from authorized sanitizer
- [ ] Receipt within validity period
- [ ] Taint flags correctly reduced after sanitization
- [ ] Partial sanitization (some taints remain) handled

### Cross-Zone Operations
- [ ] Zone crossing requires appropriate approvals
- [ ] Labels adjusted correctly on zone entry
- [ ] DecisionReceipt captures zone crossing denial

### Attack Scenarios (Adversarial)
- [ ] Taint laundering: attempt to remove taint without receipt → rejected
- [ ] Elevation bypass: low-integrity data to high-integrity zone without approval → rejected
- [ ] Declassification leak: high-confidentiality data to low-confidentiality sink → rejected
- [ ] Forged sanitizer receipt: invalid signature → rejected
- [ ] Stale approval: expired ApprovalToken → rejected

## Golden Vectors
- Provenance merge scenarios with expected output
- ApprovalToken for elevation and declassification
- SanitizerReceipt format and verification

## Property Tests
- Merge rule properties (associative, commutative)
- No taint reduction without valid receipt
- Labels never increase integrity / decrease confidentiality without approval

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "flow_type": "integrity|confidentiality",
  "from_label": "...",
  "to_label": "...",
  "approval_present": true,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All label propagation paths tested
- Approval enforcement verified
- No taint bypass possible
notes:
Added 10 property tests for provenance merge invariants:
- property_merge_commutativity
- property_merge_associativity
- property_merge_integrity_is_minimum
- property_merge_confidentiality_is_maximum
- property_merge_taint_accumulation
- property_merge_single_preserves_labels
- property_merge_empty_returns_zone_defaults
- property_taint_flags_merge_commutativity
- property_taint_flags_merge_associativity
- property_taint_flags_merge_identity

All tests use rand crate with deterministic seed for reproducibility.
close_reason: 35 comprehensive tests covering provenance fields, ApprovalToken scopes, SanitizerReceipt zone authority, taint accumulation, label merge rules, flow checks, and adversarial scenarios. All tests pass.

title: fcp.trello: Trello Kanban Board Connector
description:
# fcp.trello Connector


## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant Trello connector for boards/cards automation with strict capabilities and webhook ingestion.

Flywheel Connector Protocol implementation for Trello - the popular kanban board platform for project management and team collaboration.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in the connector’s manifest/cap map bead if present).
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)

- `operational`
- `streaming`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Request/Response + Webhook

## Overview

- **Value Score**: 63/100 (Tier 4)
- **Archetype**: Request-Response + Webhook
- **Platform**: Trello REST API
- **Model**: Simple kanban (boards, lists, cards)

## Operations

### Board Management
- Create, read, update, delete boards
- Board settings and preferences
- Board backgrounds and power-ups
- Board templates

### List Management
- Create, read, update, archive lists
- List positioning and ordering
- Move lists between boards

### Card Operations (CRUD)
- Create, read, update, delete cards
- Card positioning within lists
- Move cards between lists/boards
- Card due dates and reminders
- Card cover images

### Checklists
- Create, read, update, delete checklists
- Checklist items management
- Item completion tracking
- Copy checklists between cards

### Labels
- Create, read, update, delete labels
- Assign/remove labels from cards
- Custom label colors and names

### Attachments
- Upload attachments to cards
- Link external URLs
- Delete attachments
- Attachment previews

### Members
- List board members
- Add/remove members from boards
- Assign/unassign members to cards
- Member permissions

## Zone Architecture

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.


### Zone: board_read
- Read board details and settings
- List boards for user
- Read lists and list positions
- Read cards and card details
- Read checklists and items
- Read labels and attachments
- Read member information

### Zone: card_write
- Create/update/delete cards
- Manage card positions
- Create/update/delete checklists
- Manage checklist items
- Add/remove labels from cards
- Upload/delete attachments
- Assign/unassign members to cards

### Zone: board_admin
- Create/update/delete boards
- Create/update/delete lists
- Create/update/delete labels
- Add/remove board members
- Manage board settings
- Archive/unarchive boards

## Dependencies

- `fcp-core`: Core FCP types and traits
- `fcp-oauth`: OAuth 1.0a authentication (Trello uses OAuth 1.0a)
- `fcp-webhook`: Real-time webhook handling for board/card events
- `fcp-ratelimit`: Rate limiting (Trello: 100 requests/10s per token, 300/10s per key)
- `fcp-telemetry`: Metrics and tracing

## Technical Details

### Authentication
- OAuth 1.0a flow
- API Key + Token authentication
- Token expiration handling

### Rate Limits
- 100 requests per 10 seconds per token
- 300 requests per 10 seconds per API key
- Rate limit headers: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`

### Webhook Events
- Card created/updated/deleted
- Card moved between lists
- Checklist item completed
- Member added/removed
- Board updated
- List created/archived

### API Considerations
- Nested resources (board > list > card > checklist)
- Batch operations support
- Field selection for response optimization
- Pagination via `limit` and `before`/`since` parameters

## Implementation Plan

1. **Phase 1**: Core board and list operations
2. **Phase 2**: Card CRUD and positioning
3. **Phase 3**: Checklists and labels
4. **Phase 4**: Attachments and members
5. **Phase 5**: Webhook integration

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Full board CRUD operations
- [ ] Complete list management
- [ ] Card operations with positioning
- [ ] Checklist and label support
- [ ] Attachment upload/management
- [ ] Member management
- [ ] Webhook event handling
- [ ] Rate limit compliance
- [ ] OAuth 1.0a authentication flow
- [ ] Zone-based permission model
- [ ] Comprehensive test coverage

title: fcp.trello: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)
description:
## Goal
Produce the full FCP2 manifest and capability map for this connector.

This bead is where we translate the connector’s functional surface (operations) into a **mechanically checkable** contract:
- operation schemas + schema IDs
- capability families per operation
- per-operation NetworkConstraints (default deny)
- sandbox profile requirements
- declared state model (stateless / singleton_writer / crdt)

## Required content (must be explicit)
- Operation list is exhaustive and stable for the current phase.
- Capability IDs DO NOT encode hostnames/ports.
- NetworkConstraints are minimal and per-operation.
- Risk tier for each operation (Safe / Dangerous).
- Explicit bounds: timeouts, max response sizes, pagination limits.

## Tests
- Add good/bad manifest examples for the compliance runner.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes static checks in `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`.
- Default-deny is enforced by manifest + capability requirements.

title: fcp.trello: Provisioning Automation
description:
## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

title: fcp.twilio: Webhook handling for incoming messages
description:
# fcp.twilio: Webhook handling for incoming messages (FCP2)

## Goal
Safely ingest Twilio webhooks for:
- inbound SMS/MMS
- delivery status updates
- voice call status events (if configured)

Webhooks are a primary streaming surface and must be treated as **tainted untrusted input**.

## Scope
### Webhook ingestion
- Validate `X-Twilio-Signature` (or equivalent) against the configured webhook secret.
- Parse form-encoded payloads into typed event objects.
- Emit connector events with:
  - stable event IDs
  - correlation_id linkage

### Idempotency and replay safety
- Deduplicate repeated webhook deliveries.
- Handle out-of-order status callbacks safely.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `twilio.webhook.ingest`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Default deny: webhook ingestion itself is capability gated.
- Taint:
  - all inbound content (message body, sender number, media URLs) is tainted by default
- NetworkConstraints:
  - do not follow arbitrary media URLs unless pinned/allowed

## Deliverables
- Operation schema:
  - `twilio.webhook.ingest`
- Event envelopes:
  - `twilio.sms.incoming`
  - `twilio.sms.status`
  - `twilio.voice.status` (optional)
- Dedup state model:
  - store webhook delivery IDs / message SIDs / call SIDs as needed

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - signature validation (golden vectors)
  - taint propagation
  - dedupe/out-of-order handling
  - refusal on invalid signature

## Acceptance criteria
- Invalid signatures are rejected with a clear reason code.
- Webhook replays do not create duplicate downstream events.

title: [IDEA-01.3] SDK runtime kit: PollingSupervisor implementation
description:
## Goal
Implement the polling supervisor loop with backoff, retry taxonomy integration, and cursor hooks.

## Details
- Provide `PollingSupervisor::run()` with:
  - long-poll timeout management
  - backoff on recoverable errors
  - cursor persistence hook (does not dictate storage)
  - integration points for dedupe and per-key sequentialization
- Expose signals for rate-limit backoff (Retry-After).

## Tests
- Unit tests for backoff escalation and reset.
- Integration test with a fake polling endpoint that injects recoverable errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A reference polling connector can swap its loop for `PollingSupervisor` with minimal glue.
- Cursor progression is monotonic and never regresses after failures.
notes:
[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00

comments.author: Dicklesworthstone
comments.text: Implementation Complete - Added PollingSupervisor to runtime.rs with: PollResult enum (Success/RecoverableError/FatalError), PollingSupervisor struct with run() method, SupervisorOutcome enum, PollingSupervisorStats. Features: exponential backoff with jitter, rate-limit aware (Retry-After hints), cursor persistence hooks, health tracking integration. Added 6 new tests. Updated prelude exports. All 318 fcp-sdk tests pass, clippy clean.

title: TEST: Audit Chain Unit Tests (Hash Linking, Checkpoints, DecisionReceipts, Fork Detection)
description:
# TEST: Audit Chain Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that the audit chain provides tamper-evident history with explainable decisions.

## Test Categories

### AuditEvent Chain
- [ ] Events hash-linked correctly (prev_hash binding)
- [ ] Monotonic seq enforced
- [ ] Event types correctly discriminated
- [ ] Timestamp ordering reasonable (within skew)
- [ ] Zone binding correct
- [ ] Event payload schema-validated

### AuditHead
- [ ] Quorum signatures required (n-f for CriticalWrite)
- [ ] Insufficient signatures rejected
- [ ] Signature from non-member rejected
- [ ] Signature set canonicalized (sorted by node_id)
- [ ] AuditHead references correct chain tip

### ZoneCheckpoint
- [ ] Binds audit_head correctly (id + seq)
- [ ] Binds revocation_head correctly (id + seq)
- [ ] Binds policy_head correctly
- [ ] Binds config_head correctly
- [ ] Quorum signatures valid
- [ ] seq is monotonic

### DecisionReceipt
- [ ] Content-addressed (stable ObjectId)
- [ ] reason_code is stable and enumerable
- [ ] evidence ObjectIds are present and resolvable
- [ ] Denied operations produce DecisionReceipt
- [ ] DecisionReceipt is in audit chain

### Fork Detection
- [ ] Same seq, different id → fork detected
- [ ] Fork detection halts checkpoint advancement
- [ ] Fork emits audit event
- [ ] Fork requires manual intervention
- [ ] No auto-resolution of forks

### TraceContext Propagation
- [ ] trace_id preserved through invoke → receipt → audit
- [ ] span_id correctly propagated
- [ ] Missing trace_id handled gracefully

### Explainability (fcp explain)
- [ ] DecisionReceipt can be fetched by operation_id
- [ ] Evidence chain can be traversed
- [ ] Reason code maps to human explanation
- [ ] AI recovery hints available where applicable

## Golden Vectors
- Valid AuditEvent chain segment
- AuditHead with quorum signatures
- ZoneCheckpoint binding all heads
- DecisionReceipt with evidence

## Property Tests
- Seq is always monotonic
- Hash linking is consistent
- No event can be removed without detection

## Fuzz Targets
- AuditEvent parsing
- AuditHead signature verification
- DecisionReceipt parsing

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "chain_seq": 123,
  "event_type": "...",
  "checkpoint_seq": 456,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Chain integrity verified
- Fork detection works
- DecisionReceipts are explainable
close_reason: Added 55 comprehensive unit tests for audit chain primitives (AuditEvent, AuditHead, ZoneCheckpoint, DecisionReceipt, fork detection, TraceContext, golden vectors). All tests pass, clippy clean.

title: SymbolEnvelope AEAD encrypt/decrypt + sender subkey derivation
description:
# Goal
Implement FCP2 SymbolEnvelope AEAD encryption/decryption per spec (Section 4.1) and per-sender subkey derivation.

# Scope
- Implement per-sender subkey derivation (HKDF-SHA256 with zone_key_id salt, sender node id, sender_instance_id).
- Implement SymbolEnvelope::encrypt/decrypt with deterministic nonces and fixed-size AAD using ZoneIdHash + zone_key_id + epoch.
- Use ChaCha20-Poly1305 / XChaCha20-Poly1305 based on ZoneKeyAlgorithm.
- Return ciphertext + auth_tag separately to match envelope layout.

# Acceptance
- Unit tests: AAD construction, nonce derivation, encrypt/decrypt roundtrip for both algorithms.
- Reject mismatched zone_key_id on decrypt.
- No unsafe; secrets not logged.
notes:
[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00

title: Study: Clawdbot Discord Connector Implementation
description:
# Study: Clawdbot Discord Connector Implementation


## Goal
Study Clawdbot Discord connector implementation to extract edge cases, performance pitfalls, and security lessons for FCP2.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Objective

Research and document the existing clawdbot Discord connector implementation to extract patterns, learnings, and reusable components for building FCP connectors.

## Research Areas

### 1. Architecture Overview
- [ ] Overall connector structure
- [ ] Module organization
- [ ] Dependency graph
- [ ] Configuration management
- [ ] Entry point and initialization

### 2. Discord API Integration
- [ ] Gateway connection handling
- [ ] REST API usage patterns
- [ ] Rate limiting implementation
- [ ] Retry logic and backoff
- [ ] Error handling strategies

### 3. Event Handling
- [ ] Event subscription model
- [ ] Event parsing and validation
- [ ] Event dispatching mechanism
- [ ] Event filtering capabilities
- [ ] Async event processing

### 4. Authentication & Security
- [ ] Token management
- [ ] Permission handling
- [ ] Credential storage patterns
- [ ] Security best practices used

### 5. Message Processing
- [ ] Message parsing
- [ ] Command extraction
- [ ] Response formatting
- [ ] Attachment handling
- [ ] Embed construction

### 6. State Management
- [ ] Guild/server state tracking
- [ ] User session management
- [ ] Cache strategies
- [ ] Persistence patterns

### 7. Testing Approach
- [ ] Unit test patterns
- [ ] Integration test setup
- [ ] Mock strategies for Discord API
- [ ] Test coverage areas

## Deliverables

1. **Architecture Diagram** - Visual representation of connector structure
2. **Pattern Catalog** - Documented patterns that can be reused
3. **Anti-Pattern Notes** - What to avoid based on learnings
4. **Reusable Components List** - Code/patterns that can be extracted
5. **Recommendations** - Suggestions for FCP connector design

## Questions to Answer

- How does clawdbot handle Discord's rate limits?
- What reconnection strategy is used for the gateway?
- How are slash commands registered and handled?
- How is state synchronized across restarts?
- What logging/observability patterns are used?

## Resources

- Clawdbot repository
- Discord.js/Discord.py documentation (for comparison)
- Discord API documentation
- Existing connector documentation

## Time Estimate

- Research: 4-6 hours
- Documentation: 2-3 hours
- Total: 6-9 hours

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.
close_reason: Completed: study deliverables are documented in docs/STUDY_Discord_Connector_Implementation.md and related clawdbot pattern notes

title: fcp.jira: Automation rule management
description:
# fcp.jira: Automation rule management (FCP2)

## Goal
Support Jira Automation ("Automation for Jira") rule management for organizations that want:
- agent-assisted creation of automation rules
- safe enable/disable workflows
- auditing of automation changes

Automation configuration is **admin-level** and can have broad blast radius. This bead is explicitly treated as *dangerous*.

## Scope
### Read
- List automation rules (project-scoped / global-scoped where allowed).
- Get rule definition + status (enabled/disabled).

### Write (dangerous; approval-gated)
- Create rule (from a constrained schema).
- Update rule.
- Enable/disable rule.
- Delete rule.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `jira.automation.read`
- `jira.automation.write` (dangerous)
- `jira.admin` (dangerous; optional umbrella)

Policy guidance:
- Require ApprovalToken for any write.
- Include a two-person rule if the org policy requires it.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Receipts + audit events for all writes with:
  - rule id
  - project id
  - diff summary (structural; no secrets)

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.automation.rule.list`
  - `jira.automation.rule.get`
  - `jira.automation.rule.create`
  - `jira.automation.rule.update`
  - `jira.automation.rule.enable`
  - `jira.automation.rule.disable`
  - `jira.automation.rule.delete`
- A constrained rule representation:
  - avoid arbitrary script injection
  - prefer templates + parameterization

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- Rule CRUD happy paths.
- Permission denied.
- Validation failures (unsafe actions rejected).
- Idempotency: repeated enable/disable does not drift.

### Compliance
- Ensure all write ops require appropriate capabilities + approval.
- Ensure audit evidence includes a stable structural hash of the rule definition.

## Acceptance criteria
- Connector can manage automation rules without enabling arbitrary code execution.
- All writes are approval-gated and produce receipts/audit events.

title: fcp-webhook: Shared Webhook Handling Library
description:
# fcp-webhook: Shared Webhook Handling Library

## Overview
Production webhook handling library supporting signature verification, event routing, and delivery management for inbound webhooks.

## Signature Verification
- HMAC-SHA256 (Stripe, GitHub, Slack)
- HMAC-SHA1 (legacy)
- Ed25519 signatures
- Timestamp validation (replay prevention)
- Multiple algorithm support per provider

## Provider Support
- GitHub (X-Hub-Signature-256)
- Stripe (Stripe-Signature with timestamp)
- Slack (X-Slack-Signature)
- Linear (Linear-Signature)
- Discord (Ed25519)
- Custom providers via configuration

## Event Processing
- Event type routing
- Payload parsing (JSON, form-encoded)
- Event filtering and subscription
- Priority queue processing

## Delivery Management
- Idempotency (deduplication by event ID)
- At-least-once delivery guarantee
- Retry scheduling for failures
- Dead letter queue
- Delivery status tracking

## Security
- IP allowlist verification
- Request size limits
- Timeout enforcement
- TLS certificate validation

## Observability
- Delivery metrics
- Latency tracking
- Error categorization
- Audit logging

## Dependencies
- hmac, sha2, ed25519-dalek
- serde_json, chrono
- fcp-core, fcp-telemetry
close_reason: Implemented fcp-webhook crate with HMAC/Ed25519 signature verification, provider handlers (GitHub, Stripe, Slack, Linear), event routing, idempotency, and dead letter queue. All 18 tests passing.

title: fcp.zendesk: Auto-Categorize Incoming Tickets
description:
# fcp.zendesk: Auto-Categorize Incoming Tickets (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an automation pipeline that turns inbound tickets into structured, actionable metadata:
- category labels
- priority suggestions
- macro suggestions
- escalation flags (SLA risk, sentiment)

This bead must be **safe-by-default**: it may *recommend* actions, but must not silently mutate tickets without explicit capability + (policy) approval.

## Trigger sources
- Ticket-created / ticket-updated webhooks (tainted input).

## Scope
### Classification (suggest-only)
- Produce a `TicketClassification` object:
  - category (billing/tech/account/etc)
  - priority suggestion
  - confidence
  - recommended macros
  - recommended routing queue

### Optional apply step (dangerous)
- Apply tags/fields/macro to a ticket **only** when explicitly invoked and authorized.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `zendesk.webhook.ingest`
- `zendesk.tickets.read`
- `zendesk.classify` (produces suggestions)
- `zendesk.tickets.write` (dangerous; for applying results)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Taint:
  - ticket bodies and requester text are tainted
  - suggestions derived from tainted inputs must carry provenance/taint metadata
- For any mutation:
  - receipts + audit events
  - approval gating recommended

## Deliverables
- Event ingestion schema:
  - `zendesk.webhook.ticket_ingest`
- Operation schemas:
  - `zendesk.ticket.classify`
  - Optional: `zendesk.ticket.apply_classification`
- State:
  - webhook delivery dedupe
  - last-classified fingerprint to avoid repeated work

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - webhook replay/idempotency
  - classification object schema stability
  - deny-by-default for mutation without capability/approval

## Acceptance criteria
- Inbound tickets can be categorized deterministically with clear evidence.
- No automatic mutations occur without explicit authorization.

title: fcp.zendesk: Macro/Canned Response Management
description:
# fcp.zendesk: Macro/Canned Response Management (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable safe use of Zendesk macros/canned responses to:
- retrieve available macros
- apply macros to tickets (with controlled variable substitution)
- optionally manage macros (admin-only; dangerous)

## Scope
### Read
- List macros.
- Get macro details (actions/conditions).

### Write
- Apply macro to a ticket (dangerous-ish: mutates ticket state / adds comments).
- Optional: create/update/delete macros (dangerous; admin).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `zendesk.macros.read`
- `zendesk.macros.apply` (dangerous-ish)
- Optional: `zendesk.macros.write` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For macro apply:
  - receipts + audit events include ticket id + macro id
  - never log macro-expanded ticket content

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `zendesk.macro.list`
  - `zendesk.macro.get`
  - `zendesk.macro.apply`
  - Optional: `zendesk.macro.create/update/delete`
- Safe template handling:
  - restrict which fields can be mutated
  - sanitize substitutions

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - apply macro happy path
  - variable substitution safety
  - receipts/audit for apply

## Acceptance criteria
- Macros can be applied safely without content leakage.
- Admin macro management (if enabled) is approval gated.

title: fcp.figma: Design Collaboration and Asset Extraction Connector
description:
# fcp.figma - Design Collaboration and Asset Extraction Connector (FCP2)

## Success Criteria
- `flywheel_connectors-vuwy.1` (Manifest + Capability Map) is complete and passes static validation (IDs, schemas, NetworkConstraints, safety tiers).
- `flywheel_connectors-vuwy.2` (Provisioning Automation) is complete, minimizes human prompts, and is fully mockable for tests.
- `flywheel_connectors-vuwy.3` (Unit/Integration Tests) are mock-only, include success + denial paths, and assert log redaction (no secrets/PII).
- `flywheel_connectors-vuwy.4` (E2E Compliance Run) passes the shared compliance runner and emits an evidence bundle (logs + receipts + audit checks).
- Connector is V2-only: single-zone binding, default deny, constrained egress, audited risky/dangerous ops.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 85/100** — High priority Tier 2 connector.

**Why Critical**: Figma is the **dominant collaborative design platform** (~80% market share in product design). Enables agents to access design specs, extract assets, automate design-to-code workflows, and bridge design-development collaboration.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Design-Dev Bridge)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Knowledge,    // Design file introspection
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // Asset extraction
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Webhook events
]
```
- **Knowledge**: Design file reading, component discovery
- **Operational**: Asset export, comment management
- **Streaming**: Webhooks for file updates

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::Stateless
```
- **Stateless**: Each request is independent
- Version info fetched on demand
- No sync cursors needed

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles OAuth tokens
- Memory isolation for credential protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Figma API endpoints
    host_allow: vec\![
        "api.figma.com".into(),
        "www.figma.com".into(),
        // For image exports
        "figma-alpha-api.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com".into(),
        "s3-alpha-sig.figma.com".into(),
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// Design content is user-generated
design_content.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;

// Comments are external input
comment.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT
    | TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;

// Export URLs are external
export_url.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `figma.files.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `figma.components.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `figma.styles.read` | Safe | Design tokens |
| `figma.images.export` | Safe | Asset generation |
| `figma.comments.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `figma.comments.write` | Risky | Creates content |
| `figma.versions.read` | Safe | History |
| `figma.webhooks.manage` | Dangerous | Event routing |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Design Access
- Read file and component information
- Access design tokens and styles
- Navigate frame/component hierarchy
- Get version history

### Asset Extraction
- Export images (PNG, JPG, SVG, PDF)
- Extract component assets
- Batch export multiple nodes
- Get image fills

### Design System Integration
- Read component library info
- Access shared styles
- Get variable collections (design tokens)

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Export all icons from this file as SVGs"
"Get the design specs for this component"
"Find all text styles used in the file"
"List components in the design system"
"Extract color tokens from this file"
"Get comments on this design"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Linear/Jira Integration
**Design-task linking:**
- Link designs to issues
- Design review workflows

### GitHub Integration
- Design → code references
- PR design previews

### Slack Integration
- Design update notifications
- Review request routing

### Agent Mail Integration
- Design comments → agent threads
- Version update notifications

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **REST API**: File reading, exports
- **Webhooks**: File change events

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling
- `oauth2` — Authentication

### Authentication
| Mode | Use Case |
|------|----------|
| Personal Access Token | Individual access |
| OAuth 2.0 | Integration apps |

### Rate Limits
```rust
RateLimits {
    requests_per_minute: 720,
    // Per-file export limits apply
}
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:design:figma:full` | Full access | All files |
| `z:design:figma:team:<id>` | Team-scoped | Team files |
| `z:design:figma:readonly` | Read-only | No comments |

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `knowledge` — Design file reading
- `operational` — Asset extraction
- `streaming` — Webhook events

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.figma.com:443`
- `s3-alpha-sig.figma.com:443` (exports)

### Credential Injection
- OAuth tokens via egress proxy
- Personal tokens via CredentialId

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `figma.files.read` — File structure
- `figma.components.read` — Component info
- `figma.styles.read` — Design tokens
- `figma.versions.read` — Version history
- `figma.comments.read` — Comments

### Export Operations
- `figma.images.export` — Generate images

### Write Operations
- `figma.comments.write` — Post comments

### Admin Operations
- `figma.webhooks.manage` — Configure webhooks (Dangerous)

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Get File
```
GET /v1/files/{file_key}
```

### Get File Components
```
GET /v1/files/{file_key}/components
```

### Export Images
```
GET /v1/images/{file_key}?ids={node_ids}&format=svg&scale=2
```

Response:
```json
{
  "images": {
    "node_id_1": "https://s3-alpha-sig.figma.com/...",
    "node_id_2": "https://s3-alpha-sig.figma.com/..."
  }
}
```

### Get Comments
```
GET /v1/files/{file_key}/comments
```

### Post Comment
```json
POST /v1/files/{file_key}/comments
{
  "message": "Review feedback here",
  "client_meta": {"x": 100, "y": 200}
}
```

---

## Design System Access

### Component Libraries
```
GET /v1/teams/{team_id}/components
```

### Styles
```
GET /v1/files/{file_key}/styles
```

### Variables (Design Tokens)
```
GET /v1/files/{file_key}/variables/local
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Request construction
- Response parsing
- Node tree traversal
- Export URL handling

### Integration Tests
- Mock Figma API responses
- Multi-file scenarios
- Export workflows

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Export URL handling
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Figma API calls in unit tests
- File reading works
- Component discovery works
- Asset export works (PNG, SVG, PDF)
- Design tokens extraction works
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements satisfied

Uses X-FIGMA-TOKEN header auth, retry with backoff for 429/5xx.

title: fcp.figma: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)
description:
# fcp.figma: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the Figma connector’s **mechanical manifest contract** so the platform can sandbox, authorize, and mechanically test it under FCP2.

## Required contents
- Manifest archetypes (closed set):
  - `knowledge` (read designs, metadata, versions)
  - `operational` (comments, webhooks management)
  - `streaming` (webhook/event ingestion)
- Operation list + schema IDs.
- Capability families + per-operation required caps.
- NetworkConstraints:
  - pin Figma API host(s)
  - pin image export hosts / CDN hosts if distinct
  - deny broad egress; deny redirects by default
- State model declaration:
  - webhook subscription state
  - optional cache keys (must be bounded)

## Output artifacts
- Full manifest spec stored here.
- Good/bad examples for static compliance (especially export/download constraints).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Another engineer can implement `--manifest` from this bead alone.
- Static compliance can mechanically reject:
  - overly-broad export/download egress
  - host/port-encoded capability IDs

comments.author: Jeff Emanuel
comments.text:
Completed Figma Manifest + Capability Map:

**Manifest**: connectors/figma/manifest.toml
- Archetypes: knowledge + operational + streaming
- State model: singleton_writer (webhook subscription state, bounded cache keys)
- 12 operations: get_file, get_file_nodes, get_file_components, get_file_styles, export_images, list_file_versions, list_comments, post_comment, delete_comment, create_webhook, list_webhooks, delete_webhook
- Network: api.figma.com:443 (API), figma-alpha-api.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com + *.figma.com (CDN/export images)
- Rate limit pools: figma.read (30/min), figma.write (30/min), figma.delete (20/min), figma.export (20/min), figma.webhook (10/min)
- event_caps: streaming=true, replay=false, min_buffer_events=25
- export_images: 100MB max response, CDN hosts for image download
- Sandbox: strict, 256MB, wasi, deny_exec/deny_ptrace
- Interface hash: blake3-256:fcp.interface.v2:90cf5924...d1ba6bd0

**Test vectors**: manifest_figma_good.toml, manifest_figma_bad.toml
- Good: 3 archetypes, CDN wildcard hosts, export constraints
- Bad: system.exec duplicate + zone conflict

**Golden vector tests**: 3 tests added to manifest_golden_vectors.rs
All 70 fcp-manifest tests pass.

title: fcp.figma: Provisioning Automation (OAuth, webhooks, scopes)
description:
# fcp.figma: Provisioning Automation (OAuth, webhooks, scopes)

## Goal
Automate Figma onboarding with minimal human steps, while keeping secrets zone-scoped and never written to disk.

## Requirements
- OAuth2 (PKCE) flow support via provisioning recipes.
- Optional: Personal Access Token support (policy-gated).
- Scope selection:
  - minimal viable scopes for read operations
  - separate scopes/capabilities for comment writes and webhook management
- Webhook registration automation:
  - create/list/delete webhooks
  - verify webhook secret configuration

## Safety rules
- Validate credentials with safe read-only calls.
- Validate NetworkConstraints before declaring readiness.
- Never log access tokens.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `fcp doctor` verifies readiness (scopes + webhook reachability) without leaking secrets.
- Fully mockable for unit tests.

title: Study: Clawdbot Telegram Connector Implementation
description:
# Study: Clawdbot Telegram Connector Implementation


## Goal
Study Clawdbot Telegram connector implementation to extract edge cases, rate-limit behaviors, and security lessons for FCP2.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Objective

Research and document the existing clawdbot Telegram connector implementation to extract patterns, learnings, and reusable components for building FCP connectors.

## Research Areas

### 1. Architecture Overview
- [ ] Overall connector structure
- [ ] Module organization
- [ ] Dependency graph
- [ ] Configuration management
- [ ] Entry point and initialization

### 2. Telegram Bot API Integration
- [ ] Long polling vs webhook mode
- [ ] Update handling
- [ ] API client implementation
- [ ] Rate limiting compliance
- [ ] Error handling strategies

### 3. Event/Update Handling
- [ ] Update types supported
- [ ] Update parsing and validation
- [ ] Update dispatching mechanism
- [ ] Async processing patterns
- [ ] Update acknowledgment

### 4. Authentication & Security
- [ ] Bot token management
- [ ] User authorization patterns
- [ ] Admin permission handling
- [ ] Security best practices used

### 5. Message Processing
- [ ] Message parsing (text, commands, entities)
- [ ] Inline query handling
- [ ] Callback query handling
- [ ] Media message processing
- [ ] Keyboard/button interactions

### 6. State Management
- [ ] Conversation state tracking
- [ ] User session management
- [ ] Cache strategies
- [ ] Persistence patterns

### 7. Testing Approach
- [ ] Unit test patterns
- [ ] Integration test setup
- [ ] Mock strategies for Telegram API
- [ ] Test coverage areas

## Deliverables

1. **Architecture Diagram** - Visual representation of connector structure
2. **Pattern Catalog** - Documented patterns that can be reused
3. **Anti-Pattern Notes** - What to avoid based on learnings
4. **Reusable Components List** - Code/patterns that can be extracted
5. **Comparison with Discord** - Similarities and differences

## Questions to Answer

- How does clawdbot handle Telegram's update offset mechanism?
- What approach is used: long polling or webhooks?
- How are inline keyboards and callbacks managed?
- How is conversation context maintained?
- What logging/observability patterns are used?
- How does it compare to the Discord connector?

## Resources

- Clawdbot repository
- Telegram Bot API documentation
- Teloxide/python-telegram-bot (for comparison)
- Existing connector documentation

## Time Estimate

- Research: 4-6 hours
- Documentation: 2-3 hours
- Total: 6-9 hours

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

title: fcp.jira: Workflow transitions and status management
description:
# fcp.jira: Workflow transitions and status management (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a safe, typed interface for Jira issue workflow operations:
- discover allowed transitions
- transition issues
- query statuses/priorities/issue types relevant to workflow handling

This bead is critical for “agent takes action” flows, because transitions are the canonical state changes in Jira.

## Scope
### Read
- List available transitions for an issue (permission + workflow-driven).
- Get statuses/priorities/issue types.
- Optional: get workflow scheme metadata if permitted.

### Write
- Perform a transition on an issue, including optional field updates required by the transition.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `jira.workflow.read`
- `jira.workflow.transition` (write)
- Optional: `jira.admin` for scheme-level inspection

Policy guidance:
- Certain transitions (e.g., "Done", "Closed", "Deployed") may be policy-gated with ApprovalToken depending on org rules.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Jira base hostname.
- For transitions:
  - emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent with issue key + transition id
  - include a DecisionReceipt reason code when denied

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.workflow.transitions.list`
  - `jira.workflow.transition.apply`
  - `jira.status.list`
  - `jira.priority.list`
  - `jira.issuetype.list`
- Transition validation:
  - reject invalid transition IDs early
  - enforce required fields schema before sending

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- Transition discovery tests (different workflows).
- Transition apply tests:
  - happy path
  - missing required fields
  - forbidden
- Ensure transitions are idempotent where possible (replaying same transition should fail cleanly and not corrupt state).

### Compliance
- Ensure default deny + capability enforcement.
- Ensure all transitions produce receipts/audit.

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents can reliably move issues through workflows with explicit, auditable evidence.
- Denials are explainable via reason codes and decision receipts.

comments.text: Audited: Workflow transitions already implemented. Operations: jira.list_transitions, jira.transition_issue. Jira connector has existing unit + integration tests.

title: fcp.gitlab connector - GitLab DevOps platform integration
description:
# FCP GitLab Connector


## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant GitLab connector for repo/issues/CI automation with strict capabilities and webhook ingestion.

DevOps platform connector (GitHub alternative) for the GitLab ecosystem.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in the connector’s manifest/cap map bead if present).
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)

- `operational`
- `streaming`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Request/Response + Webhook

## Overview

The `fcp.gitlab` connector provides comprehensive integration with GitLab's DevOps platform, enabling repository management, merge request workflows, CI/CD pipeline control, container registry access, and collaboration features.

## Value Assessment

- **Value Score**: 71/100 (Tier 4)
- **Archetype**: Request-Response + Webhook
- **Priority**: P3

## Core Operations

### Repository Management
- Repository CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete/archive)
- Branch management (create, delete, protect, unprotect)
- Tag management and release creation
- Fork operations and upstream sync
- Repository mirroring configuration

### Merge Request Management
- MR creation with source/target branch specification
- Review workflows (approve, unapprove, merge, close, reopen)
- Inline comments and discussions
- MR labels, milestones, and assignees
- Draft/WIP MR handling
- Merge conflict detection and resolution hints
- Squash and merge options

### Issue Tracking
- Issue CRUD with full metadata
- Labels, milestones, weights, due dates
- Issue boards and swimlanes
- Related issues and blocking relationships
- Time tracking (estimates and spent time)
- Issue templates

### CI/CD Pipelines
- Pipeline triggering and cancellation
- Job management (retry, cancel, play manual jobs)
- Pipeline schedules
- Environment deployments
- Artifact download and browsing
- Pipeline variables and secrets (scoped)
- Multi-project pipelines
- Parent-child pipelines

### Container Registry
- Image listing and metadata
- Tag management
- Image deletion and garbage collection triggers
- Repository-level registry operations

### Wiki
- Wiki page CRUD
- Attachment handling
- Wiki export

### Snippets
- Project and personal snippets
- Multi-file snippet support
- Snippet versioning

## Zone Architecture

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.


### Zone 1: Code Read
- Repository metadata and content read
- Branch and tag listing
- MR and issue viewing
- Pipeline status viewing
- Wiki reading
- Snippet viewing

### Zone 2: Code Write
- Repository content modifications
- Branch and tag creation/deletion
- MR and issue creation/modification
- Wiki editing
- Snippet management

### Zone 3: CI/CD Control
- Pipeline triggering and management
- Job control (retry, cancel, play)
- Environment management
- Artifact access
- Registry operations

### Zone 4: Admin
- Repository settings modification
- Access level management
- Webhook configuration
- Protected branch rules
- Deploy keys and tokens
- Group/project transfer

## API Integration

### GitLab REST API v4
- Primary API for most operations
- Pagination handling (keyset and offset)
- Rate limit awareness (429 responses)
- Personal Access Token and OAuth2 authentication

### GitLab GraphQL API
- Complex queries with field selection
- Batch operations
- Efficient nested data retrieval
- Mutation support for write operations

## Dependencies

- **fcp-core**: Base connector traits and types
- **fcp-oauth**: OAuth2 authentication flow for GitLab
- **fcp-webhook**: Webhook event handling for GitLab events
- **fcp-ratelimit**: Rate limiting with GitLab-specific headers
- **fcp-telemetry**: Observability and metrics

## Webhook Events

Supported GitLab webhook event types:
- Push events
- Tag push events
- Merge request events
- Issue events
- Pipeline events
- Job events
- Deployment events
- Wiki page events
- Note (comment) events
- Release events

## Authentication Methods

1. **Personal Access Token (PAT)**: Scoped tokens for API access
2. **OAuth2**: Full OAuth2 flow for user authorization
3. **CI Job Token**: For pipeline-initiated operations
4. **Deploy Token**: Read-only access for deployments
5. **Group/Project Access Token**: Scoped to specific resources

## Rate Limiting

- Standard: 2000 requests/minute for authenticated users
- GraphQL: Complexity-based limiting
- Retry-After header support
- Automatic backoff on 429 responses

## Design Considerations

- Support for both GitLab.com and self-hosted instances
- Configurable base URL for self-hosted
- API version negotiation
- Feature flag detection for instance capabilities
- Graceful degradation for missing features

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Repository CRUD operations functional
- [ ] MR lifecycle management complete
- [ ] Issue tracking integration working
- [ ] Pipeline triggering and monitoring operational
- [ ] Container registry operations functional
- [ ] Wiki and snippet support implemented
- [ ] Webhook event handling for all event types
- [ ] OAuth2 and PAT authentication working
- [ ] Rate limiting properly handled
- [ ] Both REST and GraphQL APIs integrated
- [ ] Self-hosted instance support verified
- [ ] Zone-based permission model implemented

title: fcp.airtable: View filtering and sorting support
description:
# fcp.airtable: View Filtering and Sorting Support

## Goal
Support Airtable views as a first-class query primitive (views encode filters/sorts that humans already curate).

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.views.list`
- `airtable.views.get`
- `airtable.views.records` (list records through a view)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated (`airtable.records.read` and/or `airtable.views.read`).
- Views must be treated as “query presets”:
  - do not silently broaden access beyond what the view returns
  - enforce field selection and page size bounds
- Sorting:
  - preserve deterministic ordering when paginating
- Filter formulas:
  - validate/escape inputs to prevent injection into filter expressions

## Tests
- Mock-only tests cover:
  - pagination stability under view sorting
  - malformed view configs
  - permission failures and rate limits

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents can reliably paginate view results without duplicates.
- Filter and sort behavior matches Airtable semantics.

title: fcp.qdrant: Qdrant Vector Database Connector
description:
# fcp.qdrant - Qdrant Vector Database Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant connector for Qdrant (self-hosted or managed) supporting vector storage and semantic retrieval under strict zone isolation.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage` (CRUD: collections/points)
- `knowledge` (semantic retrieval/search)

## Zone model
- Single-zone bound: one connector instance ↔ one `ZoneId`.
- Zone policies decide which datasets are accessible; the connector must never multiplex zones.

## External surface
### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only the configured Qdrant endpoint(s) and port(s) required by declared operations.

**Important:** host restrictions are expressed via NetworkConstraints, not capability IDs.

### Credential injection
- API key / bearer token (if used) via `CredentialId`.
- Optional TLS client auth via `CredentialId` (if required by deployment).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `qdrant.collections.manage` (create/delete/update collections)
- `qdrant.points.read`
- `qdrant.points.write`
- `qdrant.search.query`
- `qdrant.snapshots.manage` (Dangerous)

## Operations (initial)
- Collections: create/get/list/delete
- Points: upsert/get/delete/scroll
- Search: query / batch query

## Retry / idempotency
- Upsert by point id is naturally idempotent; safe retries are allowed.
- Delete operations must be receipt-bound and fail closed on uncertain outcomes.

## Tests
- Mock-only unit tests using wiremock.
- E2E compliance run validates:
  - default deny
  - NetworkConstraints enforcement
  - receipts/audit for writes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic).
- No real Qdrant calls in unit tests.

title: [FCP2] DeviceProfile: Capability Reporting, Resource Constraints, Execution Fitness
description:
# [FCP2] DeviceProfile: Capability Reporting, Resource Constraints, Execution Fitness

## Goal
Define and implement the DeviceProfile schema used by the execution planner to make device-aware routing and execution decisions.

## Why This Matters
The spec envisions operations routing to devices based on capabilities (GPU, TPU, memory, battery, network). Without a concrete DeviceProfile schema, execution planning is guesswork.

## DeviceProfile Schema (NORMATIVE)

### Hardware Capabilities
```
DeviceProfile {
  node_id: TailscaleNodeId,
  profile_version: u32,
  timestamp: u64,
  
  // Compute
  cpu_cores: u16,
  cpu_arch: CpuArch,  // x86_64, aarch64, wasm32
  memory_mb: u32,
  gpu: Option<GpuProfile>,
  tpu: Option<TpuProfile>,
  
  // Storage
  local_storage_mb: u64,
  symbol_store_quota_mb: u32,
  
  // Power
  power_source: PowerSource,  // Mains, Battery(percent), Solar
  battery_percent: Option<u8>,
  
  // Network
  bandwidth_estimate_kbps: u32,
  latency_class: LatencyClass,  // Local, LAN, Internet, DERP
  metered: bool,
  
  // Availability
  availability: AvailabilityProfile,  // AlwaysOn, Scheduled, BestEffort
  next_expected_downtime: Option<u64>,
  
  // Installed Connectors
  connectors: Vec<InstalledConnector>,
}
```

### GpuProfile
```
GpuProfile {
  vendor: GpuVendor,  // Nvidia, AMD, Apple, Intel
  model: String,
  vram_mb: u32,
  compute_capability: Option<String>,  // CUDA version, etc.
}
```

### InstalledConnector
```
InstalledConnector {
  connector_id: ConnectorId,
  version: Version,
  binary_hash: ObjectId,
  capabilities: Vec<CapabilityGrant>,
}
```

## Profile Publication
- Nodes MUST publish DeviceProfile to mesh on:
  - startup
  - significant capability change
  - connector install/remove
  - periodic refresh (every 5min default)
- Profile is a canonical mesh object (content-addressed)
- Profiles are gossipped to all zone members

## Execution Fitness Scoring
The execution planner uses DeviceProfile to compute fitness:
```
fitness = base_score
  - derp_penalty (if would use DERP)
  + locality_bonus (if symbols already present)
  - battery_penalty (if on battery < 20%)
  + capability_bonus (if has required GPU/TPU)
  - latency_penalty (based on latency_class)
```

## Tests
- Unit tests: DeviceProfile serialization roundtrip
- Unit tests: fitness scoring with various profiles
- Integration tests: profile gossip propagation
- Property tests: fitness ordering is stable and deterministic

## Acceptance Criteria
- DeviceProfile schema is canonical and versioned
- Execution planner can query available profiles and compute fitness
- Profile changes trigger re-evaluation of pending operations
close_reason: Implemented DeviceProfile schema with all required types, fitness scoring, and 16 unit tests. All tests pass.

title: TEST: Tailscale Integration Unit Tests (MeshIdentity, NodeKeyAttestation, Peer Discovery)
description:
# TEST: Tailscale Integration Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate Tailscale integration (identity, attestations, peer discovery, ACL mapping) with deterministic tests/mocks.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for Tailscale mesh identity and peer discovery.

## Test Categories

### MeshIdentity
- [ ] Node ID derived from WireGuard pubkey
- [ ] Identity is stable across restarts
- [ ] Identity is unique per device
- [ ] Identity format is canonical

### NodeKeyAttestation
- [ ] Attestation binds node signing key to mesh identity
- [ ] Attestation is owner-signed
- [ ] Attestation includes timestamp
- [ ] Stale attestation detection
- [ ] Attestation verification

### Peer Discovery
- [ ] Peers discovered via Tailscale API
- [ ] Peer list updates on join/leave
- [ ] Peer health monitoring
- [ ] Peer capability detection
- [ ] Peer filtering by ACL tags

### ACL Tag Mapping
- [ ] tag:fcp-owner maps to z:owner
- [ ] tag:fcp-private maps to z:private
- [ ] tag:fcp-work maps to z:work
- [ ] tag:fcp-community maps to z:community
- [ ] tag:fcp-public maps to z:public
- [ ] Unknown tags handled gracefully

### Transport Security
- [ ] FCPS uses WireGuard encryption
- [ ] Additional AEAD layer verified
- [ ] No plaintext fallback
- [ ] Connection failures logged

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/tailscale/`
- `mesh_identity.cbor`
- `node_key_attestation.cbor`
- `peer_discovery_response.json`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_peer_discovery",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "node_id": "...",
  "peers_discovered": 3,
  "acl_tags": ["tag:fcp-work"],
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mesh identity is stable and unique
- Peer discovery works reliably
- ACL tags map correctly to zones

title: fcp.jira: Atlassian Jira Enterprise Issue Tracking Connector
description:
# fcp.jira - Atlassian Jira Enterprise Issue Tracking Connector (FCP2)

## Success Criteria
- `flywheel_connectors-x9af.1` (Manifest + Capability Map) is complete and passes static validation (IDs, schemas, NetworkConstraints, safety tiers).
- `flywheel_connectors-x9af.2` (Provisioning Automation) is complete, minimizes human prompts, and is fully mockable for tests.
- `flywheel_connectors-x9af.3` (Unit/Integration Tests) are mock-only, include success + denial paths, and assert log redaction (no secrets/PII).
- `flywheel_connectors-x9af.4` (E2E Compliance Run) passes the shared compliance runner and emits an evidence bundle (logs + receipts + audit checks).
- Connector is V2-only: single-zone binding, default deny, constrained egress, audited risky/dangerous ops.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 89/100** — High priority Tier 2 connector.

**Why Critical**: Jira dominates enterprise issue tracking with **75%+ market share** in large organizations. Many enterprises standardize on Atlassian suite. This connector enables bidirectional sync with Beads for organizations that cannot migrate away from Jira.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Enterprise Requirement)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // Issue CRUD, workflow transitions
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Webhook event ingestion
]
```
- **Operational**: Create/update/transition issues, manage sprints
- **Streaming**: Webhooks for issue changes, sprint updates

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::SingletonWriter
```
- **SingletonWriter**: Webhook event deduplication, sync cursors
- State tracked: `{ webhook_cursors: HashMap<ProjectKey, Cursor>, sync_state: SyncState }`
- Lease required for writer fencing

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles OAuth/API tokens
- Memory isolation for credential protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Jira Cloud
    host_allow: vec\![
        "*.atlassian.net".into(),
        "api.atlassian.com".into(),
        "auth.atlassian.com".into(),
    ],
    // Jira Server/DC: explicitly configured hostname
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,  // Override for Server/DC
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// Issue content from users is tainted
issue_content.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED
    | TaintFlags::PROMPT_SURFACE;

// Webhook payloads are external
webhook_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;

// Attachments and URLs are unverified
if content.contains_urls() || content.has_attachments() {
    content.provenance.taint |= TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;
}
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `jira.issues.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `jira.projects.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `jira.search` | Safe | JQL queries |
| `jira.issues.create` | Risky | Creates issues |
| `jira.issues.update` | Risky | Modifies issues |
| `jira.issues.transition` | Risky | Workflow changes |
| `jira.issues.delete` | Dangerous | Irreversible |
| `jira.projects.admin` | Dangerous | Project settings |
| `jira.webhooks.manage` | Dangerous | Event routing |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Issue Management
- Create, read, update issues across projects
- JQL query for sophisticated filtering
- Bulk operations (create, update, transition)
- Attachment handling

### Workflow Automation
- Automated issue transitions
- Sprint management
- Epic/story hierarchy navigation
- Custom field manipulation

### Reporting & Analytics
- Velocity reports
- Burndown data
- Time tracking

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Create a bug ticket for this error"
"Find all issues assigned to me"
"Move this issue to In Progress"
"Link this issue to the parent epic"
"Add a comment with the analysis"
"Close all issues in this sprint"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Linear Integration
**Issue tracking alternatives:**
- Jira ↔ Linear bidirectional sync
- Migration path support
- Enterprise compatibility

### GitHub Integration
- PR references in Jira issues
- Commit linking
- Development panel updates

### Sentry Integration
- Error → Jira issue creation
- Stack trace attachment
- Triage workflow

### Agent Mail Integration
- Issue updates → agent threads
- Assignment notifications
- Sprint status updates

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **REST API v3**: Primary CRUD operations
- **JQL**: Query language for search
- **Webhooks**: Event notifications

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling
- `oauth2` — Authentication

### Authentication Modes
| Mode | Use Case | Platform |
|------|----------|----------|
| OAuth 2.0 (3LO) | Cloud, user-delegated | **Preferred** |
| API Token | Cloud, service account | Good |
| PAT | Server/DC | Legacy |

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:work:jira:<project>` | Project-scoped | Single project |
| `z:work:jira:full` | Full access | All projects |
| `z:work:jira:readonly` | Read-only | Safe operations |

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — Issue CRUD, workflow
- `streaming` — Webhook events

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `*.atlassian.net:443` (Cloud)
- Configured hostname (Server/DC)

### Credential Injection
- OAuth tokens via egress proxy
- API tokens via CredentialId
- Webhook secrets for validation

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `jira.issues.read` — Get/list issues
- `jira.projects.read` — Project info
- `jira.search` — JQL queries
- `jira.users.read` — User info

### Write Operations
- `jira.issues.create` — Create issues
- `jira.issues.update` — Update issues
- `jira.issues.transition` — Workflow transitions
- `jira.comments.write` — Add comments

### Admin Operations
- `jira.issues.delete` — Delete issues (Dangerous)
- `jira.projects.admin` — Project settings (Dangerous)
- `jira.webhooks.manage` — Configure webhooks (Dangerous)

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Create Issue
```json
POST /rest/api/3/issue
{
  "fields": {
    "project": {"key": "PROJ"},
    "summary": "Issue title",
    "description": {"type": "doc", "content": [...]},
    "issuetype": {"name": "Bug"}
  }
}
```

### Search Issues (JQL)
```
GET /rest/api/3/search?jql=project=PROJ+AND+status=Open
```

### Transition Issue
```json
POST /rest/api/3/issue/{issueKey}/transitions
{
  "transition": {"id": "31"}
}
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Request construction
- Response parsing
- JQL query building
- Webhook signature validation

### Integration Tests
- Mock Jira API responses
- Multi-step workflows
- Error handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Jira API calls in unit tests
- Issue CRUD functional
- JQL search works
- Workflow transitions work
- Cloud and Server/DC supported
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements satisfied

comments.author: Jeff Emanuel
comments.text:
Completed Jira Manifest + Capability Map:

**Manifest**: connectors/jira/manifest.toml
- Archetypes: operational + streaming
- State model: singleton_writer (field schema / workflow caching)
- 12 operations: Issue CRUD (create/get/update/delete), JQL search, workflow transitions (list/execute), sprint management (list/move), comments (add/list), attachment upload
- Network: *.atlassian.net:443 with SNI, deny localhost/private/tailnet

- Sandbox: strict, 256MB, wasi, deny_exec/deny_ptrace

title: fcp.terraform: Plan generation and review (read-only)
description:
# fcp.terraform: Plan generation and review (read-only) (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe, read-only plan generation so agents can:
- run `terraform plan` deterministically
- parse plan output into a canonical object
- review diffs without making changes

## Scope
- Validate configuration (no mutations).
- Generate a plan artifact with:
  - stable hash
  - explicit backend/workspace context
- Render plan summary (adds/changes/destroys counts).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.plan` (read-only)
- `terraform.state.read` (may be required)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile must forbid:
  - interactive prompts
  - uncontrolled filesystem writes
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless backend/providers require explicit egress.
- Plan artifacts must be integrity-checked (hash) before any apply.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.validate`
  - `terraform.plan.generate`
  - `terraform.plan.show`
- Canonical plan object type:
  - resource change set
  - outputs diff
  - provider/version metadata

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with a terraform shim/fake fixture:
  - command args are correct (`-input=false`)
  - parsing correctness
  - bounds on stdout/stderr capture

## Acceptance criteria
- Plan generation is deterministic and safe-by-default.

title: fcp.elasticsearch: Elasticsearch Search and Analytics Connector
description:
# fcp.elasticsearch - Elasticsearch Search and Analytics Connector


## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant Elasticsearch connector for search/index management and analytics queries under strict NetworkConstraints and bounded responses.

## Overview
Elasticsearch connector for full-text search, analytics, and log aggregation.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `knowledge`
- `operational`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Search/analytics DB (Request/Response)

## Key Operations

### Document Operations
- `es.index` - Index document
- `es.get` - Get document by ID
- `es.delete` - Delete document
- `es.update` - Update document
- `es.bulk` - Bulk operations
- `es.mget` - Multi-get
- `es.reindex` - Reindex documents

### Search Operations
- `es.search` - Search documents
- `es.msearch` - Multi-search
- `es.count` - Count documents
- `es.scroll` - Scroll API for large results
- `es.point_in_time` - Point-in-time search
- `es.search_template` - Templated search
- `es.explain` - Explain query scoring

### Query Types
- match, match_phrase, multi_match
- term, terms, range
- bool (must, should, must_not, filter)
- nested, has_child, has_parent
- geo_distance, geo_bounding_box
- fuzzy, wildcard, regexp
- more_like_this

### Aggregations
- Metric: avg, sum, min, max, stats, cardinality
- Bucket: terms, histogram, date_histogram, range
- Pipeline: derivative, moving_avg, cumulative_sum
- Matrix: matrix_stats

### Index Operations
- `es.indices.create` - Create index
- `es.indices.delete` - Delete index
- `es.indices.get` - Get index info
- `es.indices.exists` - Check existence
- `es.indices.refresh` - Refresh index
- `es.indices.flush` - Flush index
- `es.indices.open/close` - Open/close index

### Mapping Operations
- `es.indices.mapping.get` - Get mapping
- `es.indices.mapping.put` - Update mapping

### Alias Operations
- `es.indices.alias.get` - Get aliases
- `es.indices.alias.add/remove` - Manage aliases

### Cluster Operations
- `es.cluster.health` - Cluster health
- `es.cluster.stats` - Cluster statistics
- `es.nodes.info` - Node information

### Analysis
- `es.indices.analyze` - Analyze text
- Custom analyzers, tokenizers, filters

## Providers Supported
- Elasticsearch (self-hosted)
- Elastic Cloud
- AWS OpenSearch
- Azure Cognitive Search (partial)

## Authentication
- Basic authentication
- API key
- Cloud ID (Elastic Cloud)
- AWS IAM (OpenSearch)

## Rate Limits
- Bulk request size limits
- Scroll context limits
- Circuit breaker settings

## Zone Architecture

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

```
Zone 0 (Public):     Cluster info
Zone 1 (Authorized): Search operations
Zone 2 (Private):    Document data
Zone 3 (Sensitive):  Admin operations, credentials
```

## Dependencies
- fcp-core, fcp-ratelimit, fcp-testkit, fcp-telemetry

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

comments.author: Jeff Emanuel
comments.text: SunnyMoose: Elasticsearch connector (fcp-elasticsearch) implemented. 7 operations: search, get_document, index_document, bulk, indices.list, indices.delete, cluster.health. API key auth + credential_id. 94 tests (60 unit + 34 integration), clippy clean.
